list.title: ApplyPatchTool
list.description: The "apply_patch" tool definition. Parses the patch into hunks, derives the new content for each add/update/move/delete (computing per-file diffs and addition/deletion counts), asks for a single "edit" permission covering all affected paths, applies the writes/removes/moves, runs the configured formatter, publishes file/edit events, refreshes the LSP and appends any diagnostics to the output.

list.title: BlockAnchorReplacer
list.description: Replacer for blocks of 3+ lines that anchors on the trimmed first and last lines, then scores candidate blocks by middle-line similarity (Levenshtein-based) and yields the best/only acceptable match's raw substring. Tolerates differences in the interior lines.

list.title: CommandPayload

list.title: CompressionMiddleware

list.title: CursorQuery
list.description: Query schema carrying an optional pagination/stream cursor.

list.title: DefaultSessionsLimit
list.description: Default number of sessions returned per page when no limit is supplied.

list.title: DiffInput
list.description: Schema for diff requests: a required sessionID and optional messageID.

list.title: DiffQuery
list.description: Query-string schema for the diff endpoint, derived from SessionSummary.DiffInput minus the path-supplied sessionID.

list.title: Disabled
list.description: Schema for an explicitly disabled LSP server entry ({ disabled: true }). Exposes a derived .zod compatibility schema.

list.title: EditTool
list.description: The "edit" tool definition. Validates the arguments, resolves the path, and under a per-file lock either creates the file (when oldString is empty) or applies a find-and-replace (preserving the file's existing line endings and BOM). It asks for "edit" permission, writes and optionally formats the file, publishes edit/update events, computes a trimmed diff plus addition/deletion counts, and appends any LSP diagnostics to the output.

list.title: Entry
list.description: Schema for a single formatter entry: optional disabled flag, command argv, environment variables, and the extensions it applies to. Exposes a derived .zod compatibility schema.

list.title: Entry
list.description: Schema for a single LSP server entry: either {@link Disabled} or an object with command, optional extensions, disabled, env, and initialization options. Exposes a derived .zod compatibility schema.

list.title: ErrorMiddleware
list.description: Express error handler. Maps known error types to HTTP status codes (NotFound -> 404, model/auth/worktree validation -> 400, else 500) and serializes them as JSON.

list.title: EscapeNormalizedReplacer
list.description: Replacer that interprets backslash escape sequences (\n, \t, \r, quotes, \, $) in the search text, then yields either the unescaped string if found directly or any content block whose own unescaping matches the unescaped search text.

list.title: Event
list.description:
Server lifecycle events published on the bus.

  • Connected: emitted when the server has connected ("server.connected").
  • Disposed: emitted on global teardown ("global.disposed").

list.title: Event
list.description: Bus event definitions for session status changes.

list.title: Event

list.title: EventRoutes

Each connection emits an initial "server.connected" event, periodic "server.heartbeat" events, and forwards all subscribed bus events until the client disconnects or the instance is disposed.

list.title: GrepDefinition
list.description: Shared grep/search tool definition Effect. Resolves the search target (file or directory), asks for grep/external-directory permission, runs the ripgrep search, collects file modification times concurrently, sorts matches newest-first, truncates to 100, and formats the matches grouped by file (long lines clamped to MAX_LINE_LENGTH).

list.title: GrepTool
list.description: The "grep" tool, built from the shared {@link GrepDefinition} (also re-exposed as "search" in tool/search.js).

list.title: History#list
list.description:
List sync events

List sync events for all aggregates. Keys are aggregate IDs the client already knows about, values are the last known sequence ID. Events with seq > value are returned for those aggregates. Aggregates not listed in the input get their full history.

list.title: HistoryEvent
list.description: Schema for a single sync event returned by the history endpoint (uses snake_case aggregate_id on the wire).

list.title: HistoryPayload
list.description: Request body schema for the history endpoint: a map of aggregate ID to the last known sequence number.

list.title: IndentationFlexibleReplacer
list.description: Replacer that matches blocks after stripping their common leading indentation, so the search text can be matched regardless of how deeply the original block is indented; yields the matching raw substring.

list.title: Info
list.description: Schema for a session's status: one of "idle", "busy", or "retry" (with attempt count, message, and next-retry timestamp).

list.title: Info
list.description: Schema for a single todo item: a content description, a status (pending/in_progress/completed/cancelled), and a priority (high/medium/low).

list.title: Info
list.description: Schema for the formatter config value: either a boolean toggle for built-ins or a record of formatter id to {@link Entry} overrides. Exposes a derived .zod schema.

list.title: Info
list.description: Schema for the lsp config value: either a boolean toggle for built-ins or a record of server id to {@link Entry}, checked by {@link requiresExtensionsForCustomServers}. Exposes a derived .zod compatibility schema.

list.title: Info
list.description: Schema for the skills section of the configuration. Carries optional additional skill folder paths and optional remote skill URLs, with a derived zod static for zod-based validation.

list.title: InitPayload
list.description: Request body schema for initializing a session: the provider/model to use and the seed message ID.

list.title: Instance#dispose

list.title: InstanceHttpApi
list.description: Instance HttpApi: all per-instance route groups (config, file, session, sync, etc.).

list.title: InstanceMiddleware
list.description: Build the instance-resolution middleware. For each request it resolves the effective workspace and adapter target, then: responds 500 for a missing workspace, proxies remote targets, or runs downstream handlers locally with the resolved (or default) directory.

list.title: InstanceMiddleware
list.description: Build middleware that establishes the workspace and instance context for a request. Resolves the target directory from the directory query param or x-closedcode-directory/x-opencode-directory headers (falling back to the process cwd), then runs the next handler inside that workspace and instance scope.

list.title: InstancePaths
list.description: URL path constants for each instance read route, keyed by endpoint name.

list.title: InstanceRoutes

list.title: InvalidError
list.description: Error raised when a parsed config fails schema validation. Payload carries the file path, optional validation issues, and an optional message.

list.title: InvalidTool
list.description: The "invalid" tool. It is never meant to be invoked deliberately ("Do not use"); it merely echoes back the validation error describing why a previous tool call's arguments were rejected.

list.title: JsonError
list.description: Error raised when a config file cannot be parsed as JSON/JSONC. Payload carries the file path and an optional parser message.

list.title: Keybinds
list.description: Zod-compatible object schema for the keybindings config, derived from {@link KeybindsSchema}; exposes .shape so consumers can parse individual fields.

list.title: KeybindsSchema
list.description: Effect Schema describing every configurable keybinding field and its default.

list.title: Layout
list.description: Schema for the layout config value, one of "auto" or "stretch". Exposes a derived .zod compatibility schema. (Deprecated: layout is always stretch.)

list.title: LineTrimmedReplacer
list.description: Replacer that matches a block of lines ignoring leading/trailing whitespace on each line, yielding the corresponding raw substring of the original content.

list.title: ListQuery
list.description: Query-string schema for listing sessions, with optional directory, scope, path, roots-only, pagination, and search filters.

list.title: LogInput
list.description: Schema for a log entry payload posted to the /log endpoint.

list.title: LoggerMiddleware

list.title: PtyRoutes
list.description: Builds the Express router for the /pty route group (list shells, PTY session list/create/get/update/remove, and a WebSocket route for real-time PTY interaction).

list.title: PublicApi
list.description: The public closedcode HttpApi, annotated with OpenAPI metadata and the legacy-compatibility spec transform.

list.title: QueryBoolean
list.description: Query-string boolean schema that decodes the strings "true"/"false" to a boolean and back.

list.title: Question#list
list.description:
List pending questions

Get all pending question requests across all sessions.

list.title: Question#reject
list.description:
Reject question request

Reject a question request from the AI assistant.

list.title: Question#reply
list.description:
Reply to question request

Provide answers to a question request from the AI assistant.

list.title: QuestionApi
list.description: HttpApi surface for the question group, exposing endpoints to list pending question requests and to reply to or reject a specific request. The group is guarded by instance-context, workspace-routing, and authorization middleware.

list.title: QuestionRoutes
list.description: Builds the Express router for the /question route group (list pending questions, reply with answers, reject).

list.title: QuestionTool
list.description: The "question" tool. Presents the supplied questions to the user via the Question service, then returns a human-readable summary of the answers (with unanswered questions marked "Unanswered") plus the raw answers in metadata.

list.title: ReadTool
list.description: The "read" tool. Resolves the requested path, checks permissions and external directory access, then renders a directory listing, an image/PDF attachment, or a paged, byte/line-capped text view; binary files are rejected. Also warms the LSP and appends any resolved instruction files as a system reminder.

list.title: ReplayEvent
list.description: Schema for a single sync event to replay, identified by event ID, aggregate ID, sequence number, type, and free-form data.

list.title: ReplayPayload
list.description: Request body schema for the replay endpoint: the target directory and a non-empty array of events to replay.

list.title: ReplayResponse
list.description: Response schema for the replay endpoint, returning the session ID produced by the replay.

list.title: ReplyPayload
list.description: Payload schema for replying to a permission request: the reply decision plus an optional message.

list.title: ReplyPayload
list.description: Request body schema for replying to a question: an array of user answers, one per question, in order.

list.title: RequestPlan
list.description: Tagged enum describing how a request should be handled: Local, Remote, or MissingWorkspace.

list.title: Resource#list
list.description:
Get MCP resources

Get all available MCP resources from connected servers. Optionally filter by name.

list.title: RevertInput
list.description: Input schema for a revert request: the session plus the message (and optional part) to revert back to.

list.title: RevertPayload
list.description: Request body schema for reverting a message, derived from SessionRevert.RevertInput minus the path-supplied sessionID.

list.title: RootHttpApi
list.description: Root (control-plane) HttpApi: control + global route groups.

list.title: SINGLE_CANDIDATE_SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD
list.description: Minimum middle-line similarity to accept a block-anchor match when there is exactly one candidate (anchors alone suffice).

list.title: SYNTHETIC_ATTACHMENT_PROMPT
list.description: Error shape thrown by fetch() when gzip/br decompression fails mid-stream

list.title: SearchArgs
list.description: Schema for the arguments passed to the Exa search tool: query, search type, result count, livecrawl mode, and optional context size cap.

list.title: SearchTool
list.description: The "search" tool: a plain alias that exposes the grep implementation under the id "search" so models that insist on calling a search tool succeed.

List sessions

Get a list of all ClosedCode sessions across projects, sorted by most recently updated. Archived sessions are excluded by default.

Abort an active session and stop any ongoing AI processing or command execution.

Create a new ClosedCode session for interacting with AI assistants and managing conversations.

list.title: Session2#delete

Delete session

Delete a session and permanently remove all associated data, including messages and history.

list.title: Session2#deleteMessage
list.description:
Delete message

Permanently delete a specific message and all of its parts from a session without reverting file changes.

list.title: Session2#diff
list.description:
Get message diff

Get the file changes (diff) that resulted from a specific user message in the session.

list.title: Session2#fork
list.description:
Fork session

Create a new session by forking an existing session at a specific message point.

list.title: Session2#get
list.description:
Get session

Retrieve detailed information about a specific ClosedCode session.

list.title: Session2#init
list.description:
Initialize session

Analyze the current application and create an AGENTS.md file with project-specific agent configurations.

list.title: Session2#list
list.description:
List sessions

Get a list of all ClosedCode sessions, sorted by most recently updated.

list.title: Session2#message
list.description:
Get message

Retrieve a specific message from a session by its message ID.

list.title: Session2#messages
list.description:
Get session messages

Retrieve all messages in a session, including user prompts and AI responses.

list.title: Session2#prompt
list.description:
Send message

Create and send a new message to a session, streaming the AI response.

list.title: Session2#promptAsync
list.description:
Send async message

Create and send a new message to a session asynchronously, starting the session if needed and returning immediately.

list.title: Session2#revert
list.description:
Revert message

Revert a specific message in a session, undoing its effects and restoring the previous state.

list.title: Session2#share
list.description:
Share session

Create a shareable link for a session, allowing others to view the conversation.

list.title: Session2#shell
list.description:
Run shell command

Execute a shell command within the session context and return the AI's response.

list.title: Session2#status
list.description:
Get session status

Retrieve the current status of all sessions, including active, idle, and completed states.

list.title: Session2#summarize
list.description:
Summarize session

Generate a concise summary of the session using AI compaction to preserve key information.

list.title: Session2#todo
list.description:
Get session todos

Retrieve the todo list associated with a specific session, showing tasks and action items.

list.title: Session2#unrevert
list.description:
Restore reverted messages

Restore all previously reverted messages in a session.

list.title: Session2#unshare
list.description:
Unshare session

Remove the shareable link for a session, making it private again.

list.title: Session2#update
list.description:
Update session

Update properties of an existing session, such as title or other metadata.

list.title: Session3#compact
list.description:
Compact v2 session

Compact a v2 session conversation.

list.title: Session3#context
list.description:
Get v2 session context

Retrieve the active context messages for a v2 session (all messages after the last compaction).

list.title: Session3#list
list.description:
List v2 sessions

Retrieve sessions in the requested order. Items keep that order across pages; use cursor.next or cursor.previous to move through the ordered list.

list.title: Session3#messages
list.description:
Get v2 session messages

Retrieve projected v2 messages for a session. Items keep the requested order across pages; use cursor.next or cursor.previous to move through the ordered timeline.

list.title: Session3#prompt
list.description:
Send v2 message

Create a v2 session message and queue it for the agent loop.

list.title: Session3#wait
list.description:
Wait for v2 session

Wait for a v2 session agent loop to become idle.

list.title: SessionApi
list.description: Experimental HttpApi surface for the session group, exposing the full set of session endpoints (list, status, get, children, todo, diff, messages, create, delete, update, fork, abort, init, share, summarize, prompt, command, shell, revert, permission response, and message/part mutations). The group is guarded by instance-context, workspace-routing, and authorization middleware.

list.title: SessionCursor
list.description: Schema describing the decoded session pagination cursor, including the active list filters so paging keeps them stable.

list.title: SessionGroup
list.description: Experimental v2 session route group, exposing endpoints to list sessions (order-based or cursor-based pagination), send a prompt, compact the conversation, wait for the agent loop to become idle, and retrieve the active context messages. Guarded by authorization middleware.

list.title: SessionID
list.description: Branded schema for session identifiers. Exposes static helpers: descending(id) to make a time-descending session ID and zod for the zod codec.

list.title: SessionListQuery
list.description: Query params for filtering the cross-project session list (directory, roots, time, search, pagination, archived).

list.title: SessionPaths
list.description: Map of endpoint name to URL path template for every session route.

list.title: SessionRestorePayload
list.description: Request body schema for restoring a session into a workspace, derived from Workspace.SessionRestoreInput minus the path-supplied workspaceID.

list.title: SessionRestoreResponse
list.description: Response schema for the session-restore endpoint, returning the total number of events scheduled for replay.

list.title: SessionRoutes
list.description: Builds the Express router for the instance /session endpoints (list, get, create, update, fork, abort, share, messages, parts, prompts, commands, revert, permissions, etc.).

list.title: ShellInput
list.description: Input schema for shell: the session, agent/model, and the command string to run.

list.title: ShellItem
list.description: Schema for a discoverable shell: its path, display name, and whether it is acceptable for use.

list.title: ShellPayload
list.description: Request body schema for running a shell command, derived from SessionPrompt.ShellInput minus the path-supplied sessionID.

list.title: ShellTool
list.description: The shell tool. Selects the configured shell, parses each command to find the file/path arguments and directories it touches (prompting for read/external access permissions), then runs the command with streamed output, byte/line truncation to a spill file, a configurable timeout, and abort handling.

list.title: SimpleReplacer
list.description: Replacer that yields the search string verbatim (exact match candidate).

list.title: SkillTool
list.description: The "skill" tool. Resolves a skill by name, asks for permission to load it, then returns the skill's markdown content plus a sampled list of its files (excluding SKILL.md) so the model can reference them by absolute path. Fails if the named skill is not found, listing the available skills.

list.title: Status
list.description: Effect Schema describing a formatter's reported status: its name, supported extensions, and whether it is enabled.

list.title: StatusMap
list.description: Schema mapping each MCP server name to its connection status.

list.title: StatusMap
list.description: Response schema mapping each session ID to its current status info.

list.title: SummarizePayload
list.description: Request body schema for summarizing a session: the provider/model to use and an optional auto flag.

list.title: Sync#replay
list.description:
Replay sync events

Validate and replay a complete sync event history.

list.title: Sync#start
list.description:
Start workspace sync

Start sync loops for workspaces in the current project that have active sessions.

list.title: SyncApi
list.description: Experimental HttpApi surface for the sync group, exposing endpoints to start workspace sync loops, replay a complete event history, and list sync events. The group is guarded by instance-context, workspace-routing, and authorization middleware.

list.title: SyncPaths
list.description: Map of endpoint name to URL path template for every sync route.

list.title: SyncRoutes
list.description: Builds the Express router for the instance /sync endpoints: start workspace syncing, replay a full sync event history, and list sync events since known sequence IDs.

list.title: TRUNCATION_DIR
list.description: Absolute path to the directory holding persisted full tool output ("tool-output" under the global data path).

list.title: TaskTool
list.description: The "task" tool. Validates the requested subagent type and permissions, creates a fresh child session (or resumes the one named by task_id), derives the model from the agent or the parent assistant message, runs the subagent prompt to completion, and returns its last text part as the result. Wires the abort signal to cancel the subagent run.

list.title: TodoItem
list.description: Schema for a single todo item: content (description), status, and priority.

list.title: TodoWriteTool
list.description: The "todowrite" tool. After requesting permission, replaces the current session's todo list with the supplied array and returns a summary of the remaining (non-completed) count plus the serialized list.

list.title: Tool#ids
list.description:
List tool IDs

Get a list of all available tool IDs, including both built-in tools and dynamically registered tools.

list.title: Tool#list
list.description:
List tools

Get a list of available tools with their JSON schema parameters for a specific provider and model combination.

list.title: ToolID
list.description: Branded ToolID schema with attached static helpers. Exposes ascending(id) to mint a new monotonically-increasing tool ID and zod for the equivalent Zod schema.

list.title: ToolID
list.description: The stable, externally-visible tool ID and permission key for the shell tool. Kept as "bash" for backwards compatibility with plugins and saved permissions.

list.title: ToolListQuery
list.description: Query params selecting the provider/model whose tools should be listed.

list.title: TrimmedBoundaryReplacer
list.description: Replacer that retries matching after trimming the search text's leading/trailing whitespace, yielding the trimmed string if found directly or any content block whose trimmed form equals it. No-ops when the search text is already trimmed.

Clear the prompt.

Submit TUI prompt

Submit the prompt.

list.title: TuiApi
list.description: Experimental HttpApi surface for the TUI group, exposing endpoints to append or submit prompts, open dialogs, show toasts, execute and publish events, select sessions, and pump the control request/response queue. The group is guarded by instance-context, workspace-routing, and authorization middleware.

list.title: TuiPaths
list.description: Map of endpoint name to URL path template for every TUI route.

list.title: TuiPublishPayload
list.description: Request body schema for the publish endpoint: any one of the supported TUI events.

list.title: TuiRequestPayload
list.description: Schema for a queued TUI control request, describing the target path and an opaque body.

list.title: TuiRoutes
list.description: Builds the Express router for the instance /tui endpoints: publishing TUI events (prompt append/submit/clear, open dialogs, toasts, command execution, session select) and the /control request/response queue bridge.

list.title: UIRoutes
list.description: Builds the Express router that serves the embedded web UI via a catch-all route.

list.title: URL
list.description: Exa MCP endpoint URL, with the EXA_API_KEY query param appended when that env var is set.

list.title: UpdatePayload
list.description: Payload schema for updating a project: optional name, icon, and commands fields.

list.title: UpdatePayload
list.description: Request body schema for updating a session: optional title, permission ruleset, and archived timestamp.

list.title: V2Api
list.description: Experimental v2 HttpApi surface, composed of the v2 session group and the v2 message group.

list.title: Vcs#diff
list.description:
Get VCS diff

Retrieve the current git diff for the working tree or against the default branch.

list.title: Vcs#get
list.description:
Get VCS info

Retrieve version control system (VCS) information for the current project, such as git branch.

list.title: VcsDiffQuery
list.description: Query schema for the VCS diff endpoint: which diff mode to compute (e.g. working tree vs default branch).

list.title: WebFetchTool
list.description: The "webfetch" tool. Validates the URL scheme, requests permission, fetches the URL with a format-aware Accept header (retrying with an honest UA when blocked by Cloudflare), enforces a 5MB response cap and timeout, and returns the body in the requested format. Images are returned as a base64 data-URL attachment.

list.title: WebSearchTool
list.description: The "websearch" tool. After requesting permission, calls the Exa web_search_exa MCP endpoint with the query and options, and returns the resulting context string (or a not-found message). The description is rendered dynamically with the current year substituted for {{year}}.

list.title: WhitespaceNormalizedReplacer
list.description: Replacer that matches by collapsing all runs of whitespace to single spaces, handling both single-line (full-line or substring) and multi-line matches, and yields the matching raw substring of the content.

list.title: Workspace#create
list.description:
Create workspace

Create a workspace for the current project.

list.title: Workspace#list
list.description:
List workspaces

List all workspaces.

list.title: Workspace#remove
list.description:
Remove workspace

Remove an existing workspace.

list.title: Workspace#sessionRestore
list.description:
Restore session into workspace

Replay a session's sync events into the target workspace in batches.

list.title: Workspace#status
list.description:
Workspace status

Get connection status for workspaces in the current project.

list.title: WorkspaceApi
list.description: Experimental HttpApi surface for the workspace group, exposing endpoints to list available workspace adapters, list/create workspaces, query connection status, remove a workspace, and restore a session's events into a workspace. The group is guarded by instance-context, workspace-routing, and authorization middleware.

list.title: WorkspacePaths
list.description: Map of endpoint name to URL path template for every workspace route.

list.title: WorkspaceRoutes
list.description: Build the Express router for the "/experimental/workspace" route group (adapter listing, workspace create/list/status/remove, and session restore).

list.title: WorkspaceRoutes~describe
list.description: Register a route's OpenAPI operation metadata under the "/experimental/workspace" base mount.

list.title: Worktree#create
list.description:
Create worktree

Create a new git worktree for the current project and run any configured startup scripts.

list.title: Worktree#list
list.description:
List worktrees

List all sandbox worktrees for the current project.

list.title: Worktree#remove
list.description:
Remove worktree

Remove a git worktree and delete its branch.

list.title: Worktree#reset
list.description:
Reset worktree

Reset a worktree branch to the primary default branch.

list.title: WriteTool
list.description: The "write" tool. Resolves the target path (relative to the instance directory if needed), guards external-directory access, computes a diff for the permission prompt, writes the content while preserving any byte-order mark, runs the configured formatter, publishes file edit/update events, and appends LSP diagnostics (for the written file and up to a few other affected files) to the output.

list.title: adapter
list.description: The Express runtime adapter implementation.

list.title: adapter.create
list.description: Build the runtime bindings for an Express app: a WebSocket upgrade helper and a listen function that starts the server with WS injection wired in.

list.title: addLegacyErrorSchemas
list.description: Add the legacy BadRequestError and NotFoundError component schemas the old SDK expected.

list.title: applyLegacySchemaOverrides
list.description: Apply hand-tuned schema overrides so specific component schemas match the legacy SDK (e.g. open additionalProperties, string Command.template, and nullable Workspace/session/provider fields).

list.title: ask
list.description: Issue the permission prompts implied by a scan result: an external-directory prompt for out-of-tree file directories and a shell-command prompt for the command patterns.

list.title: assertExternalDirectory
list.description: Promise-returning wrapper around {@link assertExternalDirectoryEffect} for callers outside the Effect runtime; runs the guard with the logger layer provided.

list.title: assertExternalDirectoryEffect
list.description: Effect that asserts a target path lies within the current instance directory, and otherwise raises an "external_directory" permission prompt for the target's containing directory. Resolves to nothing (the side effect is the permission check); does not modify the path.

list.title: attributes
list.description: Build telemetry/log attributes describing the selected backend and installation.

list.title: authorizationLayer
list.description: Layer implementing the Authorization HttpApi middleware service. Wires the basic and authToken security schemes to credential validation against ServerAuthConfig.

list.title: authorizationRouterMiddleware
list.description: Router middleware that enforces authorization for raw (non-HttpApi) routes. When auth is required, it reads the credential from the Authorization: Basic header or the auth_token query parameter (or an empty credential as a last resort) and validates it before delegating to the wrapped effect.

list.title: auto
list.description: Resolve the value of an automatic shell variable (HOME, PWD, PSHOME).

list.title: bashCommandSection
list.description: Build the bash-specific "command section" of the tool description, covering directory verification, quoting, output limits, and command chaining.

list.title: biome
list.description: Biome formatter for web/JS/TS and config/markup files. enabled(context) looks for biome.json/biome.jsonc up the tree, then resolves @biomejs/biome via npm.

list.title: bridgeRemote
list.description: Bridge an already-upgraded inbound WebSocket to a freshly-dialed outbound one. Inbound messages are queued until the outbound socket opens, then forwarded raw in both directions; close/error codes propagate across (1011 on error), with invalid close codes guarded so close() can't throw.

list.title: bridgeRemote~safeClose
list.description: Close a socket with the given code/reason, falling back to a plain close() if the code is invalid (e.g. 1005/1006), so close() never throws.

list.title: buildPrompt
list.description: Compose the compaction prompt: an anchor instruction (create-new vs update-existing), the fixed summary template, and any plugin-supplied context.

list.title: buildSpec
list.description: Build the final OpenAPI document from the registry, merged with the Effect httpapi contract's paths and components on top of a plain base document.

list.title: call
list.description: Invokes a named Exa MCP tool over HTTP and returns its first text content block.

list.title: callTui
list.description: Forwards a request to the TUI and waits for its matching response. Reads the JSON body from the context, enqueues a TUI request, then awaits the next response.

list.title: canonicalRef
list.description: Resolve a $ref to its canonical form, mapping numeric-suffixed names to their base name when that base exists.

list.title: canonicalizeSchema
list.description: Recursively canonicalize a schema for structural comparison: sort object keys, drop description, and rewrite $refs to their canonical (base) form.

list.title: cap
list.description: Clamps a delay so it never exceeds the maximum safe setTimeout value.

list.title: chainGuidance
list.description: Return guidance on how to chain dependent commands for the given shell (PowerShell 5.1 lacks &&; other shells get &&/& advice).

list.title: clang
list.description: clang-format formatter for C/C++ files. enabled(context) requires a .clang-format config up the tree and clang-format on PATH.

list.title: client
list.description:
The createClientConfig() function will be called on client initialization and the returned object will become the client's initial configuration.

You may want to initialize your client this way instead of calling setConfig(). This is useful for example if you're using Next.js to ensure your client always has the correct values.

list.title: cljfmt
list.description: cljfmt formatter for Clojure files. Enabled when cljfmt is on PATH.

list.title: closedcode/src/bus/bus-event.js
list.description: Event-type registry for the bus: defines event shapes and produces Zod / Effect schema payloads from them.

list.title: closedcode/src/bus/global.js
list.description: Process-wide singleton EventEmitter that relays bus events across instances, assigning each payload a stable id.

list.title: closedcode/src/config/console-state.js
list.description: Schema and default value for console/org-derived configuration state.

list.title: closedcode/src/config/entry-name.js
list.description: Derives a config entry name from a file path relative to known search roots.

list.title: closedcode/src/config/error.js
list.description: Named error types raised when loading or validating configuration.

list.title: closedcode/src/config/formatter.js
list.description: Config schema for code formatters (per-formatter command, env, and file extensions).

list.title: closedcode/src/config/keybinds.js
list.description: Schema and default values for all configurable TUI keybindings.

list.title: closedcode/src/config/layout.js
list.description: Config schema for the (deprecated) TUI layout mode.

list.title: closedcode/src/config/lsp.js
list.description: Config schema for LSP servers (enable/disable, command, extensions, env).

list.title: closedcode/src/config/managed.js
list.description: Reads MDM-managed configuration (config dir + macOS managed-preferences plist).

list.title: closedcode/src/config/skills.js
list.description: Config schema describing where skills are sourced from (extra paths and remote URLs).

list.title: closedcode/src/config/variable.js
list.description: Config variable interpolation: expands {env:VAR} and {file:path} tokens in config text.

list.title: closedcode/src/format/formatter.js
list.description: Registry of code formatter definitions. Each entry declares the file extensions it handles and an async enabled(context) that detects the tool/config and returns the command template (with $FILE placeholder), or false when not applicable.

list.title: closedcode/src/format/index.js
list.description: Code-formatter service: discovers enabled formatters, matches them to file extensions, and runs them on files.

list.title: closedcode/src/server/adapter.express.js
list.description: Express runtime adapter: wraps an Express app with node:http.createServer for listening, a noServer WebSocketServer (ws package) for upgrade handling (including remote-workspace WS proxying), and an in-process loopback fetch tuned for long-running agent requests.

list.title: closedcode/src/server/adapter.js
list.description: Server runtime-adapter entry point (placeholder). Resolves to the active runtime adapter (see adapter.express.js); this file is a stable shim that exports nothing on its own.

list.title: closedcode/src/server/backend.js
list.description: Server backend selection: chooses the HTTP backend (always Express) and exposes telemetry attributes for it.

list.title: closedcode/src/server/cors.js
list.description: CORS origin allow-list check for the server: permits localhost, the renderer scheme, and configured origins.

list.title: closedcode/src/server/event.js
list.description: Server lifecycle bus events: defines the Connected and Disposed events published on the global bus.

list.title: closedcode/src/server/express/app.js
list.description: Express app factory: wires the adapter, root middleware chain, shared OpenAPI registry, swagger-ui docs, and all route groups.

list.title: closedcode/src/server/express/errors.js
list.description: Shared error response schemas (400/404) for OpenAPI-documented routes, plus a helper to spread them into a route's responses.

list.title: closedcode/src/server/express/middleware.js
list.description: Server middleware: auth, logging, compression, CORS, instance resolution (with remote-workspace proxying), and error handling.

list.title: closedcode/src/server/express/openapi.js
list.description: OpenAPI foundation: collects per-route operations in a registry, converts Zod schemas to JSON Schema, merges with the Effect httpapi contract, and serves the spec via swagger-ui.

list.title: closedcode/src/server/express/validate.js
list.description: Request validation middleware: validates a request segment (param/json/query) against a Zod schema, with a 400 error envelope, plus express-validator field-chain reporting helpers.

list.title: closedcode/src/server/fence.js
list.description: Sync fence: tracks per-aggregate event sequence numbers so a remote proxy can advertise (via the x-closedcode-sync header) which aggregates advanced during a request, and block downstream reads until the local workspace has caught up to that state.

list.title: closedcode/src/server/global-lifecycle.js
list.description: Server-wide lifecycle helpers: dispose all instances and broadcast a global "disposed" event over the global bus.

list.title: closedcode/src/server/mdns.js
list.description: mDNS (Bonjour) service advertisement: publish/unpublish the server's HTTP endpoint on the local network.

list.title: closedcode/src/server/projectors.js

list.title: closedcode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/groups/file.js

list.title: closedcode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/groups/global.js

list.title: closedcode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/groups/project.js

list.title: closedcode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/groups/provider.js

list.title: closedcode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/groups/pty.js

list.title: closedcode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/handlers/experimental.js

list.title: closedcode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/handlers/file.js
list.description: HTTP API handlers for the "file" group: text/file/symbol search, directory listing, file content reads, and VCS file status.

list.title: closedcode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/handlers/global.js

list.description: HTTP API handlers for the "instance" group: dispose, path info, VCS branch/diff, and listings of commands, agents, skills, LSP servers, and formatters.

list.description: HTTP API handlers for the "permission" group: listing pending permission requests and replying to them.

list.title: closedcode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/handlers/provider.js

list.title: closedcode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/handlers/sync.js
list.description: HTTP API handlers for the "sync" group: start workspace event syncing, replay incoming events, and return event history.

list.title: closedcode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/handlers/tui.js
list.description: HTTP API handlers for the "tui" group: publish TUI events (prompt edits, command execution, toasts, session selection) onto the bus and bridge legacy control requests/responses.

list.title: closedcode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/handlers/v2.js
list.description: Aggregates the v2 HTTP API handler groups (session + message) into a single Layer backed by the SessionV2 service.

list.title: closedcode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/handlers/v2/message.js
list.description: HTTP API handlers for the "v2.message" group: paginated message listing with base64url cursor encode/decode.

list.title: closedcode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/handlers/v2/session.js
list.description: HTTP API handlers for the "v2.session" group: paginated session listing (with filter-preserving cursors) plus prompt, compact, wait, and context endpoints.

list.title: closedcode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/handlers/workspace.js
list.description: HTTP API handlers for the "workspace" group: list adapters/workspaces, create/remove workspaces, query status, and restore sessions.

list.title: closedcode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/lifecycle.js
list.description: Instance lifecycle middleware: defers disposing or reloading an instance until after the HTTP response has been produced.

list.title: closedcode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/middleware/authorization.js
list.description: HTTP API authorization middleware: optional HTTP Basic / auth-token credential validation against the configured server username and password.

list.title: closedcode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/middleware/instance-context.js
list.description: HTTP API middleware that provides the per-request instance context (resolved directory and workspace ref) from the workspace route.

list.title: closedcode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/middleware/proxy.js
list.description: HTTP and WebSocket reverse-proxy helpers used to forward instance requests to a remote workspace target.

list.title: closedcode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/middleware/workspace-routing.js
list.description: Workspace routing middleware: decide whether a request runs locally on the control plane or is proxied (HTTP/WebSocket) to a remote workspace, and provide the resolved route context.

list.title: closedcode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/public.js
list.description: Builds the public OpenAPI spec for the closedcode HTTP API, rewriting the generated spec to match the legacy (Express-generated) SDK surface.

list.title: closedcode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/server.js
list.description: Assembles the instance HTTP API server: composes auth/workspace/instance middleware, all route handlers, UI/CORS, and exposes web request handlers.

list.title: closedcode/src/server/routes/instance/middleware.js
list.description: Hono-style middleware that resolves the working directory and workspace for instance-scoped routes.

list.title: closedcode/src/server/routes/instance/trace.js
list.description: Tracing helpers for instance HTTP handlers: build OTel span attributes from a request and run handler effects inside a span.

list.title: closedcode/src/server/routes/ui.js
list.description: Shared UI-serving utilities used by the httpapi backend. The Express route group lives in routes/express/ui.js; only the Effect-based serveUIEffect helper is kept here for the httpapi server.

list.title: closedcode/src/server/workspace.js
list.description: Workspace-route resolution for the server. Decides whether an incoming request should be served locally or proxied to a remote workspace adapter, and provides the URL/session helpers that drive that decision.

list.title: closedcode/src/session/compaction.js
list.description: Session compaction: summarizes older conversation turns into an anchored Markdown summary, prunes stale tool output to reclaim context space, and (optionally) auto-continues the session after compacting on overflow.

list.title: closedcode/src/session/instruction.js
list.description: Instruction loading: discovers and reads project/global/configured instruction files (AGENTS.md and friends), fetches remote instruction URLs, and attaches nearby instruction files when the model reads source files.

list.title: closedcode/src/session/llm.js
list.description: LLM streaming service. Assembles the system prompt, provider options, tools, headers and telemetry for a request, then streams a model completion via the ai-sdk's streamText. Exposes a scoped stream Effect that aborts the underlying request when its scope closes.

list.title: closedcode/src/session/message-v2.js
list.description: Message v2 model and persistence. Defines the message/part Effect schemas and error types, converts stored messages into provider model messages (with media/compaction handling), reads/paginates messages from the database, filters out compacted history, and normalizes provider errors.

list.title: closedcode/src/session/message.js
list.description: Legacy (v1) message schema definitions. Declares the Effect/Zod schemas for message parts (text, reasoning, tool invocations, file/source), the provider error variants, and the top-level message Info shape used by the older OpenAPI surface.

list.title: closedcode/src/session/overflow.js
list.description: Context-window math for session compaction: computes the usable input budget for a model and whether current token usage overflows it.

list.title: closedcode/src/session/processor.js
list.description: Session processor: consumes the LLM stream for one assistant message, turning stream events (text/reasoning/tool/step) into persisted message parts, capturing filesystem snapshots, handling doom-loop detection, retries, and compaction signals.

list.title: closedcode/src/session/projectors-next.js
list.description: V2 session-message projectors: project SessionEvent sync events into the sqlite SessionMessage store via the synchronous SessionMessageUpdater bridged over async sequelize.

list.title: closedcode/src/session/projectors.js
list.description: Sync-event projectors that write session, message, and part changes into the sqlite store; re-exports the v2 (next) projectors.

list.title: closedcode/src/session/prompt.js
list.description: Session prompt orchestration: turns a user prompt (text/files/agents/commands/subtasks) into assistant turns, resolves tools (registry + MCP), runs the agentic step loop with compaction/title generation/structured output, and supports shell execution and slash commands.

list.title: closedcode/src/session/retry.js
list.description: Retry policy for LLM streaming: classifies retryable provider errors and computes backoff delays (honoring retry-after headers).

list.title: closedcode/src/session/revert.js
list.description: Session revert/unrevert: rolls the workspace back to a chosen message/part via filesystem snapshots, recording the resulting diff, and prunes the reverted messages on cleanup.

list.title: closedcode/src/session/run-state.js
list.description: Tracks the per-session run state: one Runner per active session, with busy/idle status reporting and cancellation.

list.title: closedcode/src/session/schema.js
list.description: Branded ID schemas (SessionID, MessageID, PartID) for the session domain, with ascending/descending ULID generators.

list.title: closedcode/src/session/status.js
list.description: Tracks and broadcasts per-session run status (idle, busy, retry) over the bus.

list.title: closedcode/src/session/summary.js
list.description: Computes and persists per-session and per-message diff summaries (additions/deletions/files) from snapshots.

list.title: closedcode/src/session/system.js
list.description: Builds the system prompt: selects the model-specific base prompt and assembles environment and skills context.

list.title: closedcode/src/session/todo.js
list.description: Persists and broadcasts a session's todo list (content, status, priority) in the database.

list.title: closedcode/src/tool/apply_patch.js
list.description: "apply_patch" tool: parses a multi-file patch into hunks, validates/permission-checks each target, then adds/updates/moves/deletes files and reports a diff plus LSP diagnostics.

list.title: closedcode/src/tool/edit.js
list.description: "edit" tool: performs a single find-and-replace edit on a file, using a cascade of fuzzy "replacer" strategies to locate the target text, then writes/formats the result and reports a diff plus LSP diagnostics.

list.title: closedcode/src/tool/external-directory.js
list.description: Guard that prompts the user for permission when a tool targets a path outside the project/worktree directory.

list.title: closedcode/src/tool/glob.js
list.description: "glob" tool: matches files by glob pattern (via ripgrep) within a directory and returns up to 100 paths sorted by modification time.

list.title: closedcode/src/tool/grep.js
list.description: "grep"/"search" tool: regex content search across files (via ripgrep), returning matches grouped by file and sorted by modification time.

list.title: closedcode/src/tool/invalid.js
list.description: Placeholder "invalid" tool: surfaces a validation error back to the model when it calls a tool with arguments that failed schema validation.

list.title: closedcode/src/tool/lsp.js
list.description: "lsp" tool: exposes Language Server Protocol queries (definition, references, hover, symbols, call hierarchy, etc.) for a file/position to the model.

list.title: closedcode/src/tool/mcp-exa.js
list.description: Client for the Exa MCP HTTP endpoint: builds JSON-RPC tools/call requests and parses the SSE response into the first text content block.

list.title: closedcode/src/tool/plan.js
list.description: The "plan_exit" tool: asks the user to approve a completed plan and, if approved, switches the session to the build agent.

list.title: closedcode/src/tool/question.js
list.description: The "question" tool: asks the user one or more questions and reports their answers back to the model.

list.title: closedcode/src/tool/read.js
list.description: The "read" tool: reads files (text, images, PDFs) and directory listings with offset/limit paging, byte/line caps, and binary detection.

list.title: closedcode/src/tool/registry.js
list.description: Tool registry service: assembles built-in, plugin, and config-directory tools and exposes them (filtered per model/agent) to the session.

list.title: closedcode/src/tool/schema.js
list.description: Schema and branded type for tool identifiers (ToolID).

list.title: closedcode/src/tool/search.js
list.description: Registers the grep implementation under the tool id "search" as an alias.

list.title: closedcode/src/tool/shell.js
list.description: The shell tool: parses commands with tree-sitter to discover file/path arguments for permission prompts, then spawns them with output streaming, truncation, timeout, and abort handling.

list.title: closedcode/src/tool/shell/id.js
list.description: Shell kind classification and the stable tool ID/permission key for the shell tool.

list.title: closedcode/src/tool/shell/prompt.js
list.description: Builds the shell tool's parameter schema and per-shell (bash/PowerShell/cmd) description prompt and usage guidance.

list.title: closedcode/src/tool/skill.js
list.description: Defines the "skill" tool, which loads a named skill's SKILL.md content and a sampled list of its accompanying files into the conversation.

list.title: closedcode/src/tool/task.js
list.description: Defines the "task" tool, which spawns (or resumes) a specialized subagent session to perform a delegated task and returns its final text result.

list.title: closedcode/src/tool/todo.js
list.description: Defines the "todowrite" tool, which replaces the current session's structured todo list.

list.title: closedcode/src/tool/tool.js
list.description: Core tool plumbing: helpers to define a tool, wrap its execute() with argument decoding, output truncation, and tracing, and lazily initialize it.

list.title: closedcode/src/tool/truncate.js
list.description: Truncate service: caps oversized tool output to line/byte limits, persists the full text to a retention-managed directory, and periodically cleans up expired files.

list.title: closedcode/src/tool/truncation-dir.js
list.description: Resolves the on-disk directory where full (truncated) tool output is persisted.

list.title: closedcode/src/tool/webfetch.js
list.description: Defines the "webfetch" tool, which fetches a URL over HTTP and returns its content as text, markdown, or html (with image handling and bot-detection retry).

list.title: closedcode/src/tool/websearch.js
list.description: Defines the "websearch" tool, which runs a web search via the Exa MCP endpoint and returns LLM-optimized result context.

list.title: closedcode/src/tool/write.js
list.description: Defines the "write" tool, which writes content to a file (preserving BOM), formats it, emits edit events, and reports LSP diagnostics.

list.title: cmd
list.description: Build the ChildProcess spec to run a command: on Windows PowerShell it invokes the shell with -Command, otherwise it runs the command through the shell's -c/shell option, detaching on non-Windows platforms.

list.title: cmdCommandSection
list.description: Build the cmd.exe-specific "command section" of the tool description, covering notes, directory verification, quoting, output limits, and chaining.

list.title: collapseDuplicateComponents
list.description: Collapse numeric-suffixed duplicate component schemas (e.g. Foo2) into their base name (Foo) when the schemas are structurally equal, rewriting all $refs and deleting the duplicate.

list.title: commandAliases
list.description: Maps legacy TUI command names to their current canonical command ids.

list.title: commands
list.description: Collect all command descendant nodes within a parse tree node.

list.title: completedCompactions
list.description: Find the prior completed compactions in a message list: each is an assistant summary message that finished without error, paired with the index of the user (compaction) message that triggered it.

list.title: componentTypeName
list.description: Convert a dotted component name into a PascalCase type name, dropping purely numeric path segments.

list.title: configEntryNameFromPath
list.description: Compute the config entry name for a file: the path after a matching search root (or the basename when none match), with its extension stripped.

list.title: configHandlers
list.description: Builds the request handlers for the "config" HttpApi group, wiring the get, update, and providers endpoints to the Config and Provider services.

list.title: configuredWorkspaceID
list.description: Read the workspace ID pinned via the CLOSEDCODE_WORKSPACE_ID flag, if any.

list.title: context
list.description: Shared, empty Effect context used as the base context for the server's request runtime.

list.title: controlHandlers
list.description: Registers the handlers for the "control" HTTP API group on the root API.

list.title: convertHTMLToMarkdown
list.description: Converts an HTML document to Markdown using Turndown, removing script, style, meta, and link elements.

list.title: convertToLineEnding
list.description: Converts LF line endings in the text to the requested ending.

list.title: cors
list.description: Build a global CORS router middleware that allows origins per the configured CORS options.

list.title: create
list.description: Validated entry point that starts a new compaction for a session. Validates input against the schema, then runs the service's create method.

list.title: createExpress
list.description: Build the Express application: wires the root middleware chain, mounts every route group against a shared OpenAPI registry, builds the OpenAPI spec, serves the docs, and registers the error handler.

list.title: createInProcessFetch
list.description: Wrap an Express app with fetch/request methods that drive it over an in-process loopback HTTP server. The lazily-started server has per-request timeouts disabled so long agent loops (run inside a single request) are not capped at 5 minutes. Does not mutate expressApp.request.

list.title: createInProcessFetch~ensureServer
list.description: Lazily start (once) the loopback HTTP server bound to 127.0.0.1.

list.title: createInProcessFetch~publicApp.fetch
list.description: Dispatch a request to the in-process server, forwarding method, headers, body, and the caller's abort signal (so explicit cancellation still propagates) via the no-timeout undici dispatcher.

list.title: createInProcessFetch~publicApp.request
list.description: Like fetch, but resolves relative URL strings against http://localhost first.

list.title: createRegistry
list.description: Create an empty per-app registry for collecting OpenAPI operations and named schemas.

list.title: createRoutes
list.description: Build the complete route layer for the server, merging root, event, instance, and UI routes and providing all required service layers (CORS, runtime, and every feature service layer).

list.title: createStructuredOutputTool
list.description: Builds the special StructuredOutput AI SDK tool used when the prompt requests JSON-schema output. The tool validates the model's arguments against the schema (with $schema stripped) and invokes onSuccess with the captured output.

list.title: createWebSocket
list.description: Build the WebSocket upgrade machinery for the Express adapter. Returns an upgradeWebSocket route-handler factory (registers per-path WS handlers) and an injectWebSocket(server) injector that handles HTTP upgrade events, dispatching to local routes or remote-workspace proxying.

list.title: createWebSocket~handleRemoteUpgrade
list.description: Proxy an inbound WebSocket upgrade to a remote-workspace target. Resolves the workspace route (env → session → ?workspace= → adapter target); only remote, currently-syncing workspaces are proxied. On any non-match it destroys the socket (no clean handshake error is possible mid-upgrade). Otherwise it completes the inbound handshake and bridges to the remote.

list.title: createWebSocket~injectWebSocket
list.description: Attach the HTTP upgrade listener to a server. Local registered routes (e.g. the pty route) take priority and are upgraded via wss; otherwise the upgrade is attempted as a remote-workspace proxy, destroying the socket on any failure.

list.title: createWebSocket~upgradeWebSocket
list.description: Create an Express middleware that registers a WS handler factory for the request's path; the actual upgrade is performed later in the server's upgrade event. Non-upgrade requests fall through.

list.title: cursor
list.description: Codec for the message pagination cursor: a base64url-encoded JSON blob carrying a message id, its creation time (epoch millis), the sort order, and the paging direction.

list.title: cursor
list.description: Opaque pagination cursor codec: encodes/decodes a { id, time } keyset cursor as a base64url string for use in message pagination.

list.title: cursor.decode
list.description: Decodes a base64url cursor string back into its validated cursor object.

list.title: cursor.decode
list.description: Decode a base64url cursor string back into a validated cursor object.

list.title: cursor.encode
list.description: Encodes a message and paging metadata into an opaque base64url cursor string.

list.title: cursor.encode
list.description: Encode a cursor object to a base64url string.

list.title: dart
list.description: dart formatter for Dart files. Enabled when dart is on PATH.

list.title: decode
list.description: URI-decode a value, returning the original input unchanged if decoding throws.

list.title: decode
list.description: Decoder that parses a JSON string into an {@link McpResult}.

list.title: decodeCredential
list.description: Decode a Base64-encoded username:password string into a credential, falling back to empty values on failure.

list.title: decodePtyID
list.description: Synchronously decodes and validates an unknown value into a PtyID, throwing on invalid input.

list.title: defaultDirectory
list.description: Resolve the project directory for the plain (non-workspace) path from the request's directory query param or x-closedcode/x-opencode-directory header, falling back to the current working directory.

list.title: defaultDirectory
list.description: Resolve the directory for a locally handled request from the directory query param, the x-closedcode-directory/x-opencode-directory headers, or the process cwd.

list.title: defaultLayer
list.description: The SessionStatus layer with its Bus dependency provided.

list.title: defaultLayer
list.description: The SessionSummary layer with all its dependencies (Session, Snapshot, Storage, Bus) provided.

list.title: defaultLayer
list.description: The SystemPrompt layer with its Skill dependency provided.

list.title: defaultLayer
list.description: The SessionTodo layer with its Bus dependency provided.

list.title: defaultLayer
list.description: The ToolRegistry layer wired with all of its default dependency layers (config, plugin, question, todo, skill, agent, session, provider, LSP, etc.).

list.title: defaultLayer
list.description: Format service Layer with its default Config and cross-spawn spawner dependencies provided.

list.title: defaultWebHandler
list.description: Lazily-constructed default web handler over routes, with deferred instance disposal as outer middleware.

list.title: define
list.description: Defines a tool. Returns an Effect (with an attached id) that resolves the tool's setup, acquires the Truncate and Agent services, and yields a record with the tool id plus a lazy init (the wrapped execute pipeline).

list.title: define
list.description: Register an event type with its property schema and remember it in the registry.

list.title: delay
list.description: Computes the wait before the next retry attempt. Prefers the provider's retry-after-ms / retry-after response headers (seconds or HTTP date); otherwise falls back to exponential backoff capped per the no-headers limit.

list.title: described
list.description: Annotate a schema with a human-readable description for OpenAPI documentation.

list.title: description
list.description: Tool description with the {{year}} placeholder replaced by the current year.

list.title: detectLineEnding
list.description: Detects the dominant line ending of a text.

list.title: dfmt
list.description: dfmt formatter for D files. Enabled when dfmt is on PATH.

list.title: diff
list.description: Compute the aggregates whose sequence number changed between two sequence maps.

list.title: dir
list.description: Resolve the base directory used to resolve relative {file:...} references.

list.title: disposeAllInstancesAndEmitGlobalDisposed
list.description: Dispose every active instance from the InstanceStore and then emit the global "disposed" event. The whole sequence is run uninterruptibly so disposal and notification cannot be torn apart.

list.title: disposeMiddleware
list.description: Outer server middleware that performs deferred instance disposal after a handler has produced its response. Looks up any instance previously marked via markInstanceForDisposal (keyed by the request source), removes the mark, and uninterruptibly runs the store disposal before returning the response.

list.title: dynamic
list.description: Detect whether a token contains dynamic/computed content (subexpressions, command substitution, or unresolved variables) that cannot be treated as a static path for permission scanning.

list.title: effectPayloads
list.description: Build Effect Schema.Struct schemas for every registered event, each annotated with an Event.<type> identifier.

list.title: embeddedUI
list.description: Resolves the lazily-loaded embedded web UI asset map.

list.title: embeddedUI
list.description: Resolve the lazily-imported embedded web UI bundle (a map of asset path to on-disk file path), or null when the embedded UI is disabled or unavailable.

list.title: emitGlobalDisposed
list.description: Effect that emits a global "disposed" event on the global bus for the "global" directory.

list.title: emptyConsoleState
list.description: A default, empty {@link ConsoleState} (no managed providers, no active org).

list.title: encodeDateTimes
list.description: Recursively converts any Effect DateTime values within a structure to epoch milliseconds so the data can be JSON-serialized into a sqlite column.

list.title: encodeMessageData
list.description: Encodes a message's data payload for storage (currently just DateTime encoding).

list.title: envValue
list.description: Look up an environment variable, matching case-insensitively on Windows.

list.title: environment
list.description: Builds the <env> system-prompt block describing the model id, working/worktree directories, VCS, platform, and date.

list.title: errors
list.description: Build a responses fragment for the given HTTP error status codes, for spreading into a route's describeRoute responses (e.g. ...errors(400, 404)).

list.title: eventApiRoutes
list.description: Layer building the event (SSE) API routes, served behind the instance router middleware.

list.title: eventData
list.description: Wraps a bus event payload into an SSE "message" frame with a JSON-encoded body.

list.title: eventData
list.description: Wraps a payload into an SSE "message" event envelope with a JSON-serialized data field.

list.title: eventHandlers
list.description: Effect HttpApi handler group wiring the "subscribe" endpoint to the SSE event response.

list.title: eventResponse
list.description: Builds the streaming SSE HTTP response: emits a server.connected event, then merges the bus event stream with a 10-second heartbeat, encoding each frame as SSE text. The stream halts when the instance is disposed.

list.title: eventResponse
list.description: Builds a Server-Sent Events HTTP response that streams global bus events plus a periodic heartbeat, starting with a server.connected event and tearing down the bus listener when the client disconnects.

list.title: expand
list.description: Expand environment-variable and automatic-variable references in a token (${env:X}, $env:X, $HOME/$PWD/$PSHOME) and then expand ~.

list.title: experimentalHandlers
list.description: Registers the handlers for the "experimental" HTTP API group on the instance API.

list.title: extract
list.description: Collect the set of file paths already loaded into context via completed, non-compacted read tool calls (from each tool result's metadata.loaded).

list.title: extractTextFromHTML
list.description: Extracts visible text from an HTML document, stripping content inside script, style, noscript, iframe, object, and embed elements.

list.title: fileHandlers
list.description: Registers the handlers for the "file" HTTP API group on the instance API.

list.title: filterCompacted
list.description: Trim a (newest-first) message list down to the active context after compaction: scans for the most recent completed compaction and drops the superseded history before its retained tail, keeping the summary and the preserved tail. Returns the result in chronological (oldest-first) order.

list.title: filterCompactedEffect
list.description: Effect variant of filterCompacted that streams a session's messages from the database, collects them, and returns the compaction-filtered list.

list.title: fixSelfReferencingComponents
list.description:
Fix component schemas that are self-referencing $refs — an Effect OpenAPI generation bug where annotated union arms that share AST nodes with other endpoints produce {"$ref":"#components/schemas/X"} as the definition of X.

Resolves by finding the actual schema from a parent union's anyOf/oneOf that references the broken component, then inlining that schema.

list.title: flattenOptions
list.description: Flatten nested anyOf/oneOf unions into a single flat list of leaf option schemas.

list.title: force
list.description: Override the selection to force a specific backend, marking the reason as "explicit" unless the forced backend already matches the current selection.

list.title: foreign
list.description: Determines whether an error is a SQLite foreign-key constraint violation, unwrapping sequelize's wrapped driver error via original if needed.

list.title: fromError
list.description: Normalize an arbitrary thrown error into one of the named message error objects (Aborted/Auth/API/ContextOverflow/OutputLength/Unknown), classifying abort exceptions, socket resets, decompression failures, and provider API-call/stream errors (including context-overflow detection).

list.title: get
list.description: Fetch a single message (with its parts) by id within a session. Throws NotFoundError when no matching message exists.

list.title: getLastModel
list.description: Find the model most recently used by a user message in the session, scanning the message stream and returning the first user message that carries a model.

list.title: getMimeType
list.description: Looks up the MIME type for a file path, normalising the library's false to undefined.

list.title: getMimeType
list.description: Look up the MIME type for a file path by extension.

list.title: getParseAs
list.description: Infers parseAs value from provided Content-Type header.

list.title: getWorkspaceRouteSessionID
list.description: Extract the session ID embedded in a /session/{id} route URL. Returns null for the /session/status route and for any URL without a session segment.

list.title: gleam
list.description: gleam formatter for Gleam files. Enabled when gleam is on PATH.

list.title: globalHandlers
list.description: Registers the handlers for the "global" HTTP API group on the root API.

list.title: gofmt
list.description: gofmt formatter for Go files. Enabled when gofmt is on PATH. enabled() returns the command array or false.

list.title: grab
list.description: Reads a field from a partial-update object, optionally descending into a nested object via cb. Returns undefined when the field is absent, but throws if the field is present yet explicitly undefined (callers must pass null to clear a value).

list.title: hasTaskTool
list.description: Determines whether the given agent is allowed to use the task tool, used to decide which truncation hint to show (delegate to an explore agent vs. read directly).

list.title: hasToolCalls
list.description: Report whether any message contains tool-call or tool-result content, used to decide if a stub tool must be injected for LiteLLM-style proxy compatibility.

list.title: headers
list.description: Build a sanitized Headers object suitable for forwarding to an upstream target. Accepts either a Request (uses its headers) or a Headers/plain-object header source, removes hop-by-hop/internal headers, then applies any extra overrides.

list.title: home
list.description: Expand a leading ~ (home directory) in a path-like token.

list.title: hop
list.description: Set of hop-by-hop header names that must not be forwarded to an upstream target.

list.title: htmlbeautifier
list.description: htmlbeautifier formatter for ERB/HTML-ERB files. Enabled when htmlbeautifier is on PATH.

list.title: http
list.description: Proxy an HTTP request to a target URL and stream the upstream response back to the client. Forwards the method, merged headers, and (for non-GET/HEAD) the request body stream; strips hop-specific content-encoding/content-length headers; aborts the upstream call when the client request is aborted; and converts errors into a plain-text 500 response.

list.title: hydrate
list.description: Attach parts to message rows: batch-loads all Part rows for the given messages (ordered), groups them by message, and returns { info, parts } objects.

list.title: info
list.description: Reconstruct a message Info object from a stored message row, hoisting the id and session id out of the JSON data column.

list.title: init
list.description: Initializes a defined tool, invoking its lazy init and merging the tool id into the resulting info record.

list.title: initProjectors
list.description: Register session projectors with the SyncEvent system, supplying a convertEvent hook that, for "session.updated" events, asynchronously loads the session row and emits its hydrated info. Other event types pass their data through unchanged.

list.title: inputUndoDefault
list.description: Default binding for input_undo, with ctrl+z prepended on Windows.

list.title: instanceApiRoutes
list.description: Layer building the full instance HttpApi from every instance-scoped handler group.

list.title: instanceContextLayer
list.description: Layer implementing the InstanceContextMiddleware HttpApi service, providing instance context via the InstanceStore.

list.title: instanceHandlers
list.description: Registers the handlers for the "instance" HTTP API group on the instance API.

list.title: instanceRouterLayer
list.description: Combined router middleware (authorization + instance context + workspace routing) for raw instance routes.

list.title: instanceRouterMiddleware
list.description: Router-level middleware that provides the instance context for raw (non-HttpApi) routes via the InstanceStore.

list.title: instanceRoutes
list.description: Combined instance routes (raw + HttpApi) with authorization, workspace routing, and instance-context middleware provided.

list.title: isAllowedCorsOrigin
list.description: Determine whether a request Origin is allowed by CORS. Always allows a missing origin, http://localhost:* and http://127.0.0.1:* ports, and the vcc://renderer scheme; otherwise allows it only if listed in opts.cors.

list.title: isAuthRequired
list.description: Determine whether authorization is enabled, i.e. a non-empty password is configured.

list.title: isBareArraySchema
list.description: Check whether a schema is a bare array type (no items or prefixItems).

list.title: isBareObjectSchema
list.description: Check whether a schema is a bare object type (no properties or additionalProperties).

list.title: isBuiltInErrorResponse
list.description: Determine whether a response is one of Effect's built-in error responses for the given error name.

list.title: isCredentialAuthorized
list.description: Check whether a credential matches the configured username and password.

list.title: isEmptyObjectUnion
list.description: Detect the degenerate "bare object OR bare array" union that Effect emits for an unconstrained record/array value.

list.title: isKind
list.description: Whether the given value is one of the recognized shell kinds.

list.title: isLocalWorkspaceRoute
list.description: Decide whether a request path/method should be served locally rather than proxied, by matching it against the static RULES table. Rules may pin a method and match a path exactly or as a prefix; the first matching rule wins.

list.title: isOverflow
list.description: Promise wrapper around the compaction service's overflow check.

list.title: isOverflow
list.description: Decide whether current token usage exceeds the model's usable budget and should trigger compaction. Always false when auto-compaction is disabled or the model has no context limit.

list.title: isPlainObject
list.description: Detects plain objects (including objects with a null prototype).

list.title: isRefResponse
list.description: Check whether a response's JSON schema is a $ref to the named component.

list.title: jsonBody
list.description: Build an OpenAPI requestBody object for an application/json endpoint.

list.title: jsonRequest
list.description: Runs an Effect generator inside an OTel span built from the request, then writes the resolved value as JSON.

list.title: jsonRequest
list.description: Runs an Effect generator inside an OTel span built from the request, then writes the resolved value as JSON.

list.title: jsonRequest
list.description: Runs an Effect generator through runRequest and writes the resolved value as JSON.

list.title: jsonRequest
list.description: Runs an Effect generator inside a request span and JSON-encodes the resolved value onto the response.

list.title: jsonRequest
list.description: Runs an Effect generator under an OTel span built from the request, then writes the resolved value as JSON.

list.title: jsonRequest
list.description: Runs an Effect generator inside an OTel span built from the request, then writes the resolved value as JSON.

list.title: jsonRequest
list.description: Runs an Effect generator inside a named span built from the request attributes, then serialises the result as JSON.

list.title: jsonRequest
list.description: Runs an Effect generator within a named span and writes the resolved value as JSON.

list.title: jsonRequest
list.description: Run a request-producing effect within a span and serialize its result as a JSON response.

list.title: jsonRequestExpress
list.description: Run an Effect generator within a traced span and write its result as the JSON response body.

list.title: keybind
list.description: Build a schema for one keybind field: an optional string that decodes to a default binding when omitted, annotated with a human-readable description.

list.title: ktlint
list.description: ktlint formatter for Kotlin files. Enabled when ktlint is on PATH.

list.title: latexindent
list.description: latexindent formatter for LaTeX files. Enabled when latexindent is on PATH.

list.title: layer
list.description: Layer that builds the SessionProcessor service, exposing a create factory for per-message processors.

list.title: layer
list.description: Layer that builds the SessionPrompt service (prompt / loop / shell / command / cancel operations).

list.title: layer
list.description: Layer that builds the SessionRevert service (revert / unrevert / cleanup operations).

list.title: layer
list.description: Layer that builds the SessionRunState service: keeps a map of session ID to Runner.

list.title: layer
list.description: Effect Layer providing the SessionStatus service, which keeps per-session status in instance state and publishes status changes on the bus.

list.title: layer
list.description: Effect Layer providing the SessionSummary service, which derives file diffs between snapshots, stores them, and exposes summarize/diff/computeDiff operations.

list.title: layer
list.description: Effect Layer providing the SystemPrompt service, which produces the environment block and the available-skills block for a prompt.

list.title: layer
list.description: Effect Layer providing the SessionTodo service, which stores todos in the database and publishes Updated events.

list.title: layer
list.description: Layer that constructs the ToolRegistry service. It discovers custom tools (from tool(s)/*.js|ts in config directories and from plugins), initializes the built-in tools, and exposes ids, all, named, and tools for resolving the active tool set for a given model/agent.

list.title: layer
list.description: Layer constructing the Truncate service. Provides cleanup, write, output, and limits operations, and forks a background fiber that purges expired truncation files once per hour (after a 1-minute startup delay).

list.title: layer
list.description: Effect Layer that constructs the Format service, wiring config and a child-process spawner into formatter discovery and execution.

list.title: legacyErrorResponse
list.description: Build a legacy error response object that references a named error schema.

list.title: levenshtein
