list.description: Levenshtein distance algorithm implementation: the minimum number of single-character edits (insertions, deletions, substitutions) needed to turn a into b.

list.title: lines
list.description: Stream a text file line by line, returning a window of lines starting at opts.offset (1-indexed) up to opts.limit lines. Individual lines longer than MAX_LINE_LENGTH are truncated, and the window is capped at MAX_BYTES; it also reports total line count and whether more content / a byte cut occurred.

list.title: listen
list.description: Start an HTTP server for the given Express app and return a control handle. Disables per-request/header timeouts only for explicit loopback binds (so long agent loops are not aborted), tries port 4096 then a random port when opts.port is 0, and lets the optional injector hook the server before it listens (used to attach WebSocket upgrade handling).

list.title: listen~start
list.description: Create and bind an http.Server on the given port (and opts.hostname), resolving with the server once it is listening.

list.title: load
list.description: Load the current event sequence number for each aggregate.

list.title: loaded
list.description: Public helper returning the set of file paths already read into context.

list.title: lock
list.description: Returns (creating if needed) the single-permit semaphore guarding edits to a given file.

list.title: locks
list.description: Per-file semaphores keyed by resolved path, used to serialize concurrent edits to the same file.

list.title: makePropertiesNullable
list.description: Recursively wrap each property schema in a nullable union, with special handling for share.url (only the url made nullable) and time (recursed into).

list.title: managedConfigDir
list.description: Get the managed config directory, honoring the CLOSEDCODE_TEST_MANAGED_CONFIG_DIR test override before falling back to the system directory.

list.title: mapNotFound
list.description: Converts a storage NotFoundError (raised as either a typed failure or a defect) into an HttpApiError.NotFound, re-dying on any other defect.

list.title: mark
list.description: Capture the services needed to dispose or reload an instance later.

list.title: markInstanceForDisposal
list.description: Mark the current request's instance to be disposed after the response is sent. Registers a pre-response handler that stashes the captured services keyed by the source Request, which disposeMiddleware later reads to perform the teardown.

list.title: markInstanceForReload
list.description: Mark the current request's instance to be reloaded after the response is sent. Registers a pre-response handler that, once the response is ready, uninterruptibly runs the store reload using the captured bridge and returns the original response unchanged.

list.title: matchLegacyOpenApi
list.description: Transform a freshly generated OpenAPI spec in place to match the legacy SDK surface. Fixes self-referencing components, strips optional-null union arms, normalizes/dedupes component names and descriptions, applies legacy schema overrides, removes built-in error/security schemas, injects instance query params, documents SSE event streams, and normalizes parameters/error responses.

list.title: maybeResolve
list.description: Resolve a schema that may be either a Zod schema or an already-built JSON Schema. Zod schemas (detected via _def/_zod) are converted; plain objects pass through.

list.title: mcpHandlers
list.description: Registers the handlers for the "mcp" HTTP API group on the instance API.

list.title: mergeEffectContractShape
list.description: Fill in missing parameters/requestBody on registry operations from the Effect httpapi contract for matching path+method pairs, mutating paths in place.

list.title: mergeOptions
list.description: Deep-merge a source options object into a target, treating a nullish source as an empty object.

list.title: messageHandlers
list.description: Builds the "v2.message" HTTP API handler group: a single "messages" endpoint that returns a page of messages plus previous/next cursors derived from the first/last items.

list.title: missingWorkspaceResponse
list.description: Build a 500 plain-text response indicating the requested workspace was not found.

list.title: mix
list.description: mix formatter for Elixir files. Enabled when mix is on PATH.

list.title: module.exports
list.description: V2 projector handlers mapping each SessionEvent sync event to a sqlite write. Most handlers forward to update to project a session.next.* message; a few (agent/model switched) also update the session row, and delta events are no-ops.

list.title: module.exports
list.description: Projector handlers for session/message/part sync events. Each SyncEvent.project pairs an event with an async handler that applies the change to the sqlite store using the dispatcher handle h = { models, sequelize, tx }. The v2 projectors (nextProjectors) are appended at the end.

list.title: module:closedcode/bus
list.description: Effect-based publish/subscribe event bus: per-instance PubSub channels (typed + wildcard) bridged to the process-wide GlobalBus, with Promise/callback helpers for non-Effect callers.

list.title: module:closedcode/bus.InstanceDisposed
list.description: Event published right before an instance's bus is shut down, carrying the instance directory.

list.title: module:closedcode/bus.createID
list.description: Generate a new ascending event id.

list.title: module:closedcode/bus.defaultLayer
list.description: Default layer used when no explicit Bus layer is provided.

list.title: module:closedcode/bus.layer
list.description: Effect layer that builds the Bus service: per-instance state plus publish/subscribe operations.

list.title: module:closedcode/bus.publish
list.description: Publish an event from non-Effect code (resolves once published).

list.title: module:closedcode/bus.subscribe
list.description: Subscribe to a single event type with a callback from non-Effect code.

list.title: module:closedcode/bus.subscribeAll
list.description: Subscribe to all event types with a callback from non-Effect code.

list.title: module:closedcode/cli
list.description: CLI entry point. Builds the yargs command tree (run, serve, tui, models, providers, etc.), initializes logging, runs the one-time JSON-to-SQLite database migration on first launch, and dispatches the parsed command.

list.title: module:closedcode/cli~show
list.description: Write help/usage output to stderr, prefixing it with the logo unless it is already a closedcode usage string.

list.title: module:closedcode/config
list.description: Configuration schema and loader. Defines the closedcode config shape and merges layered sources (global, project .closedcode, env) into the effective configuration consumed across the app.

list.title: module:closedcode/config.ConfigDirectoryTypoError
list.description: Named error raised when a config directory path appears to be a typo, carrying the offending path, directory, and a suggested correction.

list.title: module:closedcode/config.Info
list.description: The canonical Effect Schema describing the full closedcode configuration shape. Carries a derived .zod strict-object compatibility surface for Express validators.

list.title: module:closedcode/config.Layout
list.description: Zod-compatible schema for the (deprecated) layout config value.

list.title: module:closedcode/config.Server
list.description: Zod-compatible schema for the server config section.

list.title: module:closedcode/config.defaultLayer
list.description: The config {@link layer} with all of its dependency layers (filesystem, env, auth, account, npm, flock) provided, ready to be used standalone.

list.title: module:closedcode/config.layer
list.description: Effect Layer that builds the {@link Service}: loads and merges global, custom, project-local, console/org, and managed config sources, resolves plugins, and exposes accessors (get, getGlobal, getConsoleState, directories, waitForDependencies) and mutators (update, updateGlobal, invalidate).

list.title: module:closedcode/config/agent
list.description: Agent/mode config schema and loaders that parse markdown definitions from disk.

list.title: module:closedcode/config/agent.Info
list.description: Decoded agent config schema that applies normalize on decode and a Zod equivalent via statics.

list.title: module:closedcode/config/agent.load
list.description: Load all agent definitions under a directory by scanning {agent,agents}/**\/*.md, parsing each markdown file's frontmatter and body, and decoding it into an agent config. Parse failures are logged and published as a session error event and skipped rather than aborting the whole load.

list.title: module:closedcode/config/agent.loadMode
list.description: Load all mode definitions under a directory by scanning {mode,modes}/*.md, parsing each markdown file, and decoding it as an agent config forced into mode: "primary". Parse failures are logged and published as a session error event; entries that fail schema decoding are skipped.

list.title: module:closedcode/config/agent~AgentSchema
list.description: Raw agent config schema as authored, allowing arbitrary extra keys (normalized later).

list.title: module:closedcode/config/agent~Color
list.description: Schema for an agent color: a six-digit hex code or a named theme color.

list.title: module:closedcode/config/agent~KNOWN_KEYS
list.description: The set of recognized top-level agent config keys; any other key is folded into options.

list.title: module:closedcode/config/agent~normalize
list.description: Normalize a raw agent config: promote unknown keys into options, translate the deprecated tools boolean map into a permission object (write/edit/patch collapse into permission.edit), merge any explicit permission on top, and coalesce steps ?? maxSteps.

list.title: module:closedcode/config/command
list.description: Command config schema and loader that parses markdown command definitions from disk.

list.title: module:closedcode/config/command.Info
list.description: Schema for a user-defined command (template plus optional description, agent, model, subtask flag).

list.title: module:closedcode/config/command.load
list.description: Load all command definitions under a directory by scanning {command,commands}/**\/*.md, parsing each markdown file's frontmatter and body (the body becomes the command template), and validating against the command schema. Markdown parse failures are logged and published as a session error event and skipped; a file that parses but fails schema validation throws an InvalidError.

list.title: module:closedcode/config/markdown
list.description: Parsing helpers for markdown instruction files: extracts @file references and !`shell` directives from templates and parses YAML frontmatter (with a permissive fallback for malformed YAML).

list.title: module:closedcode/config/markdown.FILE_REGEX
list.description: Matches @path file references in a template (not preceded by a word char or backtick).

list.title: module:closedcode/config/markdown.FrontmatterError
list.description: Error thrown when a markdown file's YAML frontmatter cannot be parsed.

list.title: module:closedcode/config/markdown.SHELL_REGEX
list.description: Matches !`command` shell directives in a template.

list.title: module:closedcode/config/markdown.fallbackSanitization
list.description: Sanitize a document's YAML frontmatter so a stricter parser can read it. Leaves comments, blank lines, indented continuations, empty/quoted/block-scalar values untouched, and converts key: value pairs whose value contains a colon into a block scalar (key: |-) so the inner colon does not break YAML parsing.

list.title: module:closedcode/config/markdown.files
list.description: Find all @path file references in a template.

list.title: module:closedcode/config/markdown.parse
list.description: Read and parse a markdown instruction file, returning its frontmatter and body. Attempts a strict parse first, then retries through {@link fallbackSanitization} for files with malformed YAML frontmatter.

list.title: module:closedcode/config/markdown.shell
list.description: Find all !`command` shell directives in a template.

list.title: module:closedcode/config/mcp
list.description: Effect schemas describing MCP (Model Context Protocol) server config: local (spawned command) and remote (URL, optional OAuth) server definitions.

list.title: module:closedcode/config/mcp.Info
list.description: Discriminated union (on type) of any MCP server config, local or remote.

list.title: module:closedcode/config/mcp.Local
list.description: Schema for a local MCP server launched by running a command on the host.

list.title: module:closedcode/config/mcp.OAuth
list.description: Schema for OAuth authentication settings used by a remote MCP server.

list.title: module:closedcode/config/mcp.Remote
list.description: Schema for a remote MCP server reached over HTTP, with optional OAuth.

list.title: module:closedcode/config/model-id
list.description: Effect schema for a model identifier config value (a plain string).

list.title: module:closedcode/config/model-id.ConfigModelID
list.description: Schema for a model ID config value; a bare string with a derived zod static.

list.title: module:closedcode/config/parse
list.description: Config parsing helpers: JSONC parsing with rich error reporting, plus validation of parsed data against both zod and Effect schemas.

list.title: module:closedcode/config/parse.effectSchema
list.description: Validate and decode data against an Effect schema, preserving the user's original property order and rejecting unrecognized top-level keys.

list.title: module:closedcode/config/parse.jsonc
list.description: Parse JSONC (JSON with comments and trailing commas) text into a value. On parse failure, throws a {@link JsonError} whose message includes the input and a per-error report with line/column and a caret pointing at the problem.

list.title: module:closedcode/config/parse.schema
list.description: Validate and parse data against a zod schema.

list.title: module:closedcode/config/parse~topLevelExtraKeys
list.description: List top-level keys present in data that are not declared by the schema. Returns an empty array unless data is a plain object and the schema is a closed object type (no index signatures), since only then are extra keys meaningful.

list.title: module:closedcode/config/paths
list.description: Config file discovery. Resolves where config files live for a project, walking up from the working directory to the worktree root plus the global config directory, and reads individual config files tolerantly.

list.title: module:closedcode/config/paths.directories
list.description: Effect that resolves the deduplicated list of config directories to consult: the global config dir, any .closedcode dirs found between directory and the worktree root (unless project config is disabled) plus one in the home dir, and an explicit override directory when configured.

list.title: module:closedcode/config/paths.fileInDirectory
list.description: Build the candidate .json and .jsonc paths for a config file in a directory.

list.title: module:closedcode/config/paths.files
list.description: Effect that finds <name>.jsonc/<name>.json config files by walking up from a directory to the worktree root, returned outermost-first (root before nested).

list.title: module:closedcode/config/paths.readFile
list.description: Read a config file, returning undefined for missing files and throwing JsonError for other failures.

list.title: module:closedcode/config/permission
list.description: Effect schemas for permission config: the ask/allow/deny actions, per-target rule maps, and the per-tool permission input (with shorthand normalization) used to decide which operations require confirmation.

list.title: module:closedcode/config/permission.Action
list.description: Schema for a single permission decision: "ask", "allow", or "deny".

list.title: module:closedcode/config/permission.Info
list.description: Schema for a fully-decoded permission config; accepts the shorthand or object input and decodes to the per-tool object form.

list.title: module:closedcode/config/permission.Object
list.description: Schema for a map of target pattern to {@link Action} (per-target rules).

list.title: module:closedcode/config/permission.Rule
list.description: Schema for a permission rule: either a single {@link Action} or an {@link Object} of per-target actions.

list.title: module:closedcode/config/permission~normalizeInput
list.description: Normalize a permission input into its object form.

list.title: module:closedcode/config/plugin
list.description: Plugin config schemas and helpers: discovers local plugin files in a directory, reads a plugin spec's identifier and options, resolves path-like specs relative to the declaring config file, and deduplicates plugins by load identity.

list.title: module:closedcode/config/plugin.Options
list.description: Schema for arbitrary inline plugin options (a string-keyed record of unknown values).

list.title: module:closedcode/config/plugin.Spec
list.description: Schema for a plugin spec: a plain identifier string, or a [identifier, options] tuple.

list.title: module:closedcode/config/plugin.deduplicatePluginOrigins
list.description: Deduplicate plugins by load identity, keeping the last-declared occurrence of each (package name for npm specs, exact file URL for local specs) while preserving the original ordering and each entry's full provenance.

list.title: module:closedcode/config/plugin.load
list.description: Discover local plugin files under plugin/ or plugins/ in a directory.

list.title: module:closedcode/config/plugin.pluginOptions
list.description: Extract the inline options of a plugin spec, if any.

list.title: module:closedcode/config/plugin.pluginSpecifier
list.description: Extract the identifier portion of a plugin spec.

list.title: module:closedcode/config/plugin.resolvePluginSpec
list.description: Resolve a path-like plugin spec into an absolute file:// URL (or its package entry), anchored to the config file that declared it. Non-path specs (e.g. npm package names) are returned unchanged.

list.title: module:closedcode/config/provider
list.description: Effect schemas describing model-provider config: per-model metadata (cost, limits, modalities, capabilities, variants) and the provider-level config (API/npm settings, model whitelist/blacklist, request options).

list.title: module:closedcode/config/provider.Info
list.description: Schema for a provider's config: endpoint/package identity, model allow/deny lists, request options, and per-model overrides.

list.title: module:closedcode/config/provider.Model
list.description: Schema for a single model's metadata: identity, capabilities, cost, limits, modalities, and variants.

list.title: module:closedcode/config/server
list.description: Effect schema for the HTTP server config: listen port/hostname, mDNS service discovery, and additional allowed CORS domains.

list.title: module:closedcode/config/server.Server
list.description: Schema for the server config: listen address, mDNS discovery, and CORS allowances.

list.title: module:closedcode/config~globalConfigFile
list.description: Resolve the path of the global config file, preferring an existing closedcode.jsonc/closedcode.json/config.json under the global config dir, and falling back to the first candidate when none exist.

list.title: module:closedcode/config~mergeConfig
list.description: Deep-merge two config objects, with source values overriding target.

list.title: module:closedcode/config~mergeConfigConcatArrays
list.description: Deep-merge two config objects but union their instructions arrays instead of letting source replace target's array.

list.title: module:closedcode/config~normalizeLoadedConfig
list.description: Strip deprecated TUI-related keys (theme, keybinds, tui) from a loaded config record, logging a deprecation warning when any are present.

list.title: module:closedcode/config~patchJsonc
list.description: Apply a (possibly nested) patch object onto a JSONC document string while preserving comments and formatting, recursing through record values to build the JSON path for each leaf edit.

list.title: module:closedcode/config~resolveLoadedPlugins
list.description: Resolve each plugin spec in a loaded config relative to the file that declared it, so path-like specs (e.g. ./plugin.mjs) keep their original base location.

list.title: module:closedcode/config~writable
list.description: Produce a writable copy of a config object by dropping the internal plugin_origins provenance metadata that should never be persisted.

list.title: module:closedcode/config~writableGlobal
list.description: Produce a writable copy of a config object for persisting to the global file, additionally clearing an empty-string shell so a blank key is not written.

list.title: module:closedcode/node
list.description: Public Node entry barrel. Re-exports the core building blocks consumed by embedders: configuration, the HTTP server, the bootstrap routine, logging, the database handle and the JSON-to-SQLite migration.

list.title: module:closedcode/provider
list.description: Provider registry and model resolution. Loads provider definitions, merges config/auth/env overrides, and exposes helpers — including the locality checks that enforce closedcode's local-only egress policy — used to instantiate models.

list.title: module:closedcode/provider.ConfigProvidersResult
list.description: Schema for the config-providers response: the config-defined providers and their default models.

list.title: module:closedcode/provider.Info
list.description: Schema for a resolved provider, including its source, env var names, key, and models.

list.title: module:closedcode/provider.InitError
list.description: Error: the provider SDK could not be constructed (wraps the underlying cause).

list.title: module:closedcode/provider.ListResult
list.description: Schema for the /provider list response: all providers, default model per provider, and connected ids.

list.title: module:closedcode/provider.Model
list.description: Schema for a fully-resolved model (merged from models.dev, config, env, and auth).

list.title: module:closedcode/provider.ModelNotFoundError
list.description: Error: the requested provider or model id was not found (carries fuzzy suggestions).

list.title: module:closedcode/provider.defaultLayer
list.description: The Provider layer with all its service dependencies provided.

list.title: module:closedcode/provider.defaultModelIDs
list.description: Pick a default model id for each provider — the top of {@link sort}'s ordering. Providers with no models are skipped (rather than crashing) so they still list.

list.title: module:closedcode/provider.fromModelsDevProvider
list.description: Build an internal provider record from a models.dev provider, expanding each model and adding synthetic <id>-<mode> entries for any experimental modes (merging their cost/body/header overrides on top of the base model).

list.title: module:closedcode/provider.isLocalProvider
list.description: Determine whether a provider talks only to local endpoints, checking its base URL and every model's API URL via {@link isLocalURL}.

list.title: module:closedcode/provider.isLocalURL
list.description: Determine whether a URL points at the local machine or a private network. Treats localhost, loopback, RFC 1918 / link-local IPv4, ULA / loopback / link-local IPv6, and .local / .lan / .internal hostnames as local.

list.title: module:closedcode/provider.parseModel
list.description: Split a "provider/model" string into branded provider and model ids (the model portion may itself contain slashes, which are rejoined).

list.title: module:closedcode/provider.sort
list.description: Sort models into a preferred display order: by priority family (qwen/llama/gpt-oss) first, then "latest"-tagged ids, then id descending.

list.title: module:closedcode/provider/auth
list.description: Provider authentication service. Collects plugin-supplied auth methods (OAuth and API-key flows with their interactive prompts) and drives the authorize/callback handshake, persisting the resulting credentials via Auth.

list.title: module:closedcode/provider/auth.AuthorizeInput
list.description: Input to the authorize step: the chosen method index and any prompt answers.

list.title: module:closedcode/provider/auth.CallbackInput
list.description: Input to the callback step: the method index and (for "code" flows) the OAuth code.

list.title: module:closedcode/provider/auth.Methods
list.description: Map from provider id to the list of auth methods it supports.

list.title: module:closedcode/provider/auth.OauthCallbackFailed
list.description: Error: the provider's OAuth callback did not return a successful result.

list.title: module:closedcode/provider/auth.OauthCodeMissing
list.description: Error: a "code" OAuth flow reached callback without an authorization code.

list.title: module:closedcode/provider/auth.OauthMissing
list.description: Error: callback was called for a provider with no pending authorization.

list.title: module:closedcode/provider/auth.ValidationFailed
list.description: Error: a prompt input failed the provider's validation.

list.title: module:closedcode/provider/auth.defaultLayer
list.description: The ProviderAuth layer with its Auth and Plugin dependencies provided.

list.title: module:closedcode/provider/auth.layer
list.description: Layer building the ProviderAuth service. Reads auth hooks from loaded plugins and exposes methods (list available methods), authorize (start an OAuth flow), and callback (finish the flow and persist credentials).

list.title: module:closedcode/provider/auth~Prompt
list.description: Union of the supported interactive auth prompt kinds.

list.title: module:closedcode/provider/auth~SelectOption
list.description: A single choice in a {@link SelectPrompt}.

list.title: module:closedcode/provider/auth~SelectPrompt
list.description: A single-choice auth prompt presenting a fixed set of options.

list.title: module:closedcode/provider/auth~TextPrompt
list.description: A free-text auth prompt (e.g. asking the user for an API base URL).

list.title: module:closedcode/provider/auth~When
list.description: Conditional that shows/hides a prompt based on another input's value (eq/neq).

list.title: module:closedcode/provider/error
list.description: Provider error normalization. Classifies provider/LLM API errors into a common shape (e.g. context_overflow vs api_error), extracts human-readable messages from heterogeneous response bodies, and detects context-window overflow across many providers via message-pattern matching.

list.title: module:closedcode/provider/error.parseAPICallError
list.description: Normalize a non-streaming API call error into a common shape, classifying it as "context_overflow" (via {@link isOverflow}, a 413, or an explicit context_length_exceeded code) or otherwise "api_error" with status/retry info.

list.title: module:closedcode/provider/error.parseStreamError
list.description: Parse an error object delivered inside a streaming response into a normalized error, mapping known error codes (context_length_exceeded, insufficient_quota, usage_not_included, invalid_prompt, server_error) to their type and retryability.

list.title: module:closedcode/provider/error~isOpenAiErrorRetryable
list.description: Decide whether an OpenAI-style API error should be retried, treating 404 as retryable because OpenAI sometimes 404s models that are actually available.

list.title: module:closedcode/provider/error~isOverflow
list.description: Detect whether an error message indicates a context-window overflow, matching the provider-specific {@link OVERFLOW_PATTERNS} plus bare 400/413 "(no body)" responses from providers like Cerebras and Mistral.

list.title: module:closedcode/provider/error~json
list.description: Coerce a value to a plain object: parse a JSON string, pass through objects, and return undefined for anything that is not (or does not parse to) an object.

list.title: module:closedcode/provider/error~message
list.description: Build the best human-readable message for a provider error, falling back from the error message to the response body, JSON error fields, or the HTTP status text, and rewriting HTML gateway/proxy 401/403 pages into actionable guidance.

list.title: module:closedcode/provider/models
list.description: models.dev database service. Defines the schema for provider/model metadata (costs, limits, capabilities) and loads it from a configured on-disk path or the build-time generated snapshot, caching the result indefinitely.

list.title: module:closedcode/provider/models.Model
list.description: Schema for a single model's metadata in the models.dev database.

list.title: module:closedcode/provider/models.Provider
list.description: Schema for a provider entry in the models.dev database (its models keyed by id).

list.title: module:closedcode/provider/models.defaultLayer
list.description: The ModelsDev layer with its AppFileSystem dependency provided.

list.title: module:closedcode/provider/models.layer
list.description: Layer building the ModelsDev service. Loads the model database from the CLOSEDCODE_MODELS_PATH file if set, else the bundled build-time snapshot (else {}), and caches it forever — exposing get and a refresh invalidator.

list.title: module:closedcode/provider/models~Cost
list.description: Per-token cost schema, with an optional override for >200k-token contexts.

list.title: module:closedcode/provider/schema
list.description: Branded string schemas for provider and model identifiers, with helpers (zod conversion, and pre-made ids for the local lmstudio/ollama providers).

list.title: module:closedcode/provider/schema.ModelID
list.description: Branded model id schema; statics include the zod converter.

list.title: module:closedcode/provider/schema.ProviderID
list.description: Branded provider id schema; statics include zod, lmstudio, and ollama constants.

list.title: module:closedcode/provider/transform
list.description: Provider request-transform helpers. Adapts messages and request options to a model's declared capabilities (filtering unsupported attachments, deriving temperature/reasoning variants, capping output tokens) before a request is sent.

list.title: module:closedcode/provider/transform.OUTPUT_TOKEN_MAX
list.description: Hard cap on output tokens (overridable via CLOSEDCODE_EXPERIMENTAL_OUTPUT_TOKEN_MAX).

list.title: module:closedcode/provider/transform.ProviderTransform
list.description: Aggregated namespace of the provider-transform helpers.

list.title: module:closedcode/provider/transform.maxOutputTokens
list.description: Compute the max output tokens for a request: the model's output limit clamped to {@link OUTPUT_TOKEN_MAX} (falling back to that cap when no limit is set).

list.title: module:closedcode/provider/transform.message
list.description: Filter message file parts down to modalities the model can actually accept, dropping attachments whose MIME type maps to an unsupported (or unknown) modality.

list.title: module:closedcode/provider/transform.options
list.description: Build the request options bag, spreading the caller's provider options and nesting them under the providerOptions namespace via {@link providerOptions}.

list.title: module:closedcode/provider/transform.providerOptions
list.description: Wrap provider options under the openaiCompatible namespace expected by the

list.title: module:closedcode/provider/transform.schema
list.description: Transform an output JSON schema for a model (currently a pass-through).

list.title: module:closedcode/provider/transform.smallOptions
list.description: Build the default options for the small/auxiliary model (empty provider options).

list.title: module:closedcode/provider/transform.temperature
list.description: Resolve the temperature to send for a model: 0 when supported, undefined otherwise.

list.title: module:closedcode/provider/transform.topK
list.description: Resolve the top-k value to send for a model (currently always unset).

list.title: module:closedcode/provider/transform.topP
list.description: Resolve the top-p value to send for a model (currently always unset).

list.title: module:closedcode/provider/transform.variants
list.description: Build the set of reasoning-effort variants ("low"/"medium"/"high") for a model that supports reasoning; returns an empty object when reasoning is unsupported.

list.title: module:closedcode/provider/transform~mimeToModality
list.description: Map a MIME type to the modality category used by model capability checks.

list.title: module:closedcode/provider~cost
list.description: Convert a models.dev cost record into the internal cost shape, defaulting missing fields to 0 and carrying over the optional >200k-context override.

list.title: module:closedcode/provider~fromModelsDevModel
list.description: Build an internal Model from a models.dev provider+model pair, deriving API info, capabilities/modalities, cost, limits, and reasoning-effort variants.

list.title: module:closedcode/provider~isPrivateIPv4
list.description: Determine whether a host string is a private/loopback/link-local IPv4 address (loopback 127/8, RFC 1918 10/8, 172.16/12, 192.168/16, and link-local 169.254/16).

list.title: module:closedcode/provider~isPrivateIPv6
list.description: Determine whether a host string is a loopback/private/link-local IPv6 address (::1 loopback, fc00::/7 unique-local, fe80::/10 link-local).

list.title: module:closedcode/provider~layer
list.description: Layer building the Provider service. Resolves the full provider/model database by merging the models.dev snapshot with plugin hooks, config, environment variables, and stored auth — enforcing the local-only policy by dropping any non-local provider — and exposes the lookup/SDK-resolution operations.

list.title: module:closedcode/provider~wrapSSE
list.description: Wrap a Server-Sent-Events Response so that a stalled stream is aborted if no chunk arrives within ms. Returns the response unchanged for non-positive timeouts, bodyless responses, or non-SSE content types.

list.title: module:closedcode/server
list.description: HTTP server assembly. Wires the Express backend, request adapter, OpenAPI description and mDNS advertisement together into the closedcode server that the SDK and TUI connect to.

list.title: module:closedcode/server.Legacy
list.description: Build an Express server, forcing the explicit "express" backend selection.

list.title: module:closedcode/server.listen
list.description: Build and start the server, listening on the configured hostname/port, and optionally publishing an mDNS advertisement (skipped for loopback hosts). Sets the module-level url to the resolved listen address.

list.title: module:closedcode/server.openapi
list.description: Return the OpenAPI description document for the Express backend.

list.title: module:closedcode/server~create
list.description: Build a fresh Express server with the auto-selected backend and given options.

list.title: module:closedcode/server~select
list.description: Determine which server backend to use.

list.title: module:closedcode/server~withBackend
list.description: Log the chosen server backend selection and pass the built server through.

list.title: module:closedcode/session
list.description: Session domain model. Creates and persists chat sessions, tracks parent / child relationships and usage, maps between storage rows and runtime objects, and publishes session lifecycle events on the bus.

list.title: module:closedcode/session.Event
list.description: Session-related events. Created/Updated/Deleted are sync events persisted via projectors; Diff and Error are bus-only events.

list.title: module:closedcode/session.fromRow
list.description: Converts a sqlite session row into a runtime session Info object, decoding nested summary/share/model/time fields and normalizing nulls to undefined.

list.title: module:closedcode/session.getUsage
list.description: Computes token usage and cost for a model step. Normalizes provider usage fields (subtracting cached tokens from input, separating reasoning tokens) and applies the model's per-million pricing, including the optional over-200K tier.

list.title: module:closedcode/session.isDefaultTitle
list.description: Tests whether a title is an auto-generated default (prefix + ISO timestamp).

list.title: module:closedcode/session.layer
list.description: Layer that builds the Session service: CRUD over sessions, messages, and parts.

list.title: module:closedcode/session.listGlobal
list.description: Async-iterates sessions across all projects (not scoped to the current instance), applying optional directory/roots/time-range/search/archived filters and joining each session with its project summary.

list.title: module:closedcode/session.plan
list.description: Computes the absolute path of a session's plan markdown file. Stored under the worktree's .closedcode/plans when the project is under version control, otherwise under the global data directory.

list.title: module:closedcode/session.toRow
list.description: Converts a runtime session Info object into a sqlite row, flattening nested summary/share/time fields to their column names.

list.title: module:closedcode/session~createDefaultTitle
list.description: Builds a default session title from a timestamped prefix.

list.title: module:closedcode/session~db
list.description: Runs an async database callback inside an Effect, providing the sequelize handle.

list.title: module:closedcode/session~getForkedTitle
list.description: Derives a forked-session title, incrementing an existing (fork #N) suffix or appending (fork #1) when none is present.

list.title: module:closedcode/session~listByProject
list.description: Queries sessions for a project, applying optional workspace/path/directory, roots-only, start time, and title-search filters, ordered by most recently updated.

list.title: module:closedcode/session~plain
list.description: Extracts a plain JS object from a sequelize row instance.

list.title: module:closedcode/session~sessionPath
list.description: Computes a session's directory path relative to the worktree, normalized to forward slashes.

list.title: module:sdk
list.description: Top-level SDK entry point. Re-exports the client and server factories and provides {@link createOpencode}, a convenience helper that boots a server and returns a client already pointed at it.

list.title: module:sdk.createOpencode
list.description: Start a closedcode server and return a client connected to it.

list.title: module:sdk/client
list.description: SDK HTTP client factory. Builds an {@link OpencodeClient} that talks to a running closedcode server, injecting the active directory as a request header / query parameter so server-side resolution stays scoped to the caller's project.

list.title: module:sdk/client.createClosedcodeClient
list.description: Create a typed client for a running closedcode server.

list.title: module:sdk/client~pick
list.description: Return value unless it is just an (encoded) restatement of fallback, in which case the canonical fallback is preferred.

list.title: module:sdk/client~rewrite
list.description: Move the x-closedcode-directory hint onto the URL as a directory query parameter for GET/HEAD requests (which cannot carry a body), then strip the header so it is not sent twice.

list.title: module:sdk/process
list.description: Child-process lifecycle helpers for the SDK: terminate a spawned process (with a Windows process-tree fallback) and wire an AbortSignal to a process.

list.title: module:sdk/process.bindAbort
list.description: Bind an AbortSignal to a child process so that aborting stops the process, and so that the listeners are cleaned up automatically once the process exits or errors.

list.title: module:sdk/process.stop
list.description: Terminate a child process if it is still running. On Windows the whole process tree is killed via taskkill /T /F, falling back to proc.kill() elsewhere or when taskkill fails.

list.title: module:sdk/server
list.description: SDK process launchers. Spawns the closedcode binary either as a headless HTTP server ({@link createOpencodeServer}) or as an interactive TUI ({@link createOpencodeTui}), passing configuration via env and forwarding aborts.

list.title: module:sdk/server.createOpencodeServer
list.description: Spawn a headless closedcode server process and resolve once it is listening.

list.title: module:sdk/server.createOpencodeTui
list.description: Spawn an interactive closedcode TUI process that inherits the current stdio.

list.title: module:sdk/v2
list.description: v2 SDK entry point. Re-exports the v2 client/server factories and the data helpers, and provides {@link createOpencode} to boot a server and return a client already connected to it.

list.title: module:sdk/v2.createOpencode
list.description: Start a closedcode server and return a v2 client connected to it.

list.title: module:sdk/v2/client
list.description: v2 SDK HTTP client factory. Like {@link module:sdk/client} but also threads an experimental workspace id alongside the project directory onto outgoing requests, and rejects responses that indicate an incompatible server version.

list.title: module:sdk/v2/client.createClosedcodeClient
list.description: Create a typed v2 client for a running closedcode server.

list.title: module:sdk/v2/client~pick
list.description: Return value unless it is merely an (optionally encoded) restatement of fallback, in which case the canonical fallback is preferred.

list.title: module:sdk/v2/client~rewrite
list.description: Move the x-closedcode-directory / x-closedcode-workspace hints onto the URL as directory / workspace query parameters for GET/HEAD requests, then strip the headers so they are not sent twice.

list.title: module:sdk/v2/data
list.description: v2 SDK data builders. Helpers for constructing message payloads in the shape the server expects (info envelope plus parts linked back to the message).

list.title: module:sdk/v2/data~message
list.description: Builders for message objects.

list.title: module:sdk/v2/data~message.user
list.description: Build a user message from input, splitting it into an info envelope and a list of parts that reference the message and session ids.

list.title: module:sdk/v2/server
list.description: v2 SDK process launchers. Spawns the closedcode binary as a headless HTTP server ({@link createOpencodeServer}) or as an interactive TUI ({@link createOpencodeTui}), passing configuration via env and forwarding aborts.

list.title: module:sdk/v2/server.createOpencodeServer
list.description: Spawn a headless closedcode server process and resolve once it is listening.

list.title: module:sdk/v2/server.createOpencodeTui
list.description: Spawn an interactive closedcode TUI process that inherits the current stdio.

list.title: nextTuiRequest
list.description: Pulls the next pending TUI request from the request queue.

list.title: nixfmt
list.description: nixfmt formatter for Nix files. Enabled when nixfmt is on PATH.

list.title: normalizeComponentDescriptions
list.description: Replace component descriptions with the curated legacy descriptions, deleting descriptions for all others.

list.title: normalizeComponentNames
list.description: Rename dotted component names to PascalCase type names, merging into an existing target when structurally equal or renaming otherwise, and rewriting all $refs accordingly.

list.title: normalizeLegacyErrorResponses
list.description: Replace built-in 400/404 error responses on an operation with the legacy error response shapes.

list.title: normalizeLegacyOperation
list.description: Apply per-route legacy operation tweaks: drop spurious error responses on specific endpoints and give the message/command POST endpoints their explicit {info, parts} 200 response shape.

list.title: normalizeLineEndings
list.description: Converts all CRLF line endings in the text to LF.

list.title: normalizeParameter
list.description: Normalize an operation parameter's schema for the legacy SDK: apply path/query schema overrides, coerce known number/boolean query params, and otherwise strip optional-null arms.

list.title: normalizePath
list.description: Normalize an Express route path to OpenAPI form: ":param" becomes "{param}" and a trailing slash is dropped except on the root path.

list.title: nullable
list.description: Wrap a schema in an anyOf: [schema, {type:"null"}] union, unless it is already nullable.

list.title: ocamlformat
list.description: ocamlformat formatter for OCaml files. enabled(context) requires ocamlformat on PATH and a .ocamlformat config up the tree.

list.title: older
list.description: Build a Sequelize WHERE clause selecting rows strictly older than a cursor, ordered by creation time then id (the keyset-pagination tiebreaker).

list.title: openapi
list.description: Build a fresh Express app and return its OpenAPI spec, without listening.

list.title: operations
list.description: Supported LSP operation names accepted by the tool's operation parameter.

list.title: ormolu
list.description: ormolu formatter for Haskell files. Enabled when ormolu is on PATH.

list.title: oxfmt
list.description: oxfmt formatter for JS/TS files (gated behind the experimental flag). enabled(context) requires the flag and an oxfmt (dev)dependency in a nearby package.json.

list.title: page
list.description: Fetch one page of a session's messages (newest-first internally, returned oldest-first) using keyset pagination, hydrating each with its parts. Throws NotFoundError when the session does not exist and no rows are returned.

list.title: paramToAttributeKey
list.description: Map a route-param name to an OpenTelemetry attribute key: a "fooID" param becomes "foo.id", any other key becomes "closedcode.".

list.title: paramToAttributeKey
list.description: Normalises an Express route param name into an OTel attribute key: fooID becomes foo.id; any other param is namespaced under closedcode..

list.title: paramToAttributeKey
list.description: Normalises an Express route param name into an OTel attribute key: fooID becomes foo.id; any other param is namespaced under closedcode..

list.title: paramToAttributeKey
list.description: Normalises an Express route param name into an OTel attribute key: fooID becomes foo.id; any other param is namespaced under closedcode..

list.title: paramToAttributeKey
list.description: Normalises a route param name into an OTel span attribute key.

list.title: paramToAttributeKey
list.description: Map a route param name to an OTel span attribute key. Names ending in ID become <lowercased-prefix>.id (e.g. sessionID -> session.id); all other names are namespaced under closedcode. to avoid colliding with standard conventions.

list.title: parameterSchema
list.description: Build the shell tool's parameter schema (command, optional timeout, optional workdir, and a required description) using the supplied description text for the description field.

list.title: parse
list.description: Parse the sync fence header into a sequence map. Returns undefined when the header is missing or invalid; otherwise keeps only entries with a string key and an integer sequence value.

list.title: parse
list.description: Parse a command string into a tree-sitter syntax tree using the bash or PowerShell grammar.

list.title: parseBody
list.description: Parses a JSON request body, defaulting an empty body to {}.

list.title: parseManagedPlist
list.description: Strip MDM/plist metadata keys from a JSON-encoded managed plist, returning the remaining closedcode config as a JSON string.

list.title: parseSse
list.description: Parses a Server-Sent Events response body, decoding each data: line as an MCP result and returning the first non-empty text content found.

list.title: parser
list.description: Lazily initialize tree-sitter and load the bash and PowerShell grammars once, returning the two configured parsers.

list.title: part
list.description: Reconstruct a message Part object from a stored part row, hoisting the id, session id, and message id out of the JSON data column.

list.title: parts
list.description: Load all parts of a single message in id order, reconstructed as Part objects.

list.title: parts
list.description: Extract the meaningful tokens (command name and argument-like elements) of a tree-sitter command node, skipping separators and redirections.

list.title: pathArgs
list.description: Extract the path-like arguments from a command's token list, skipping the command name, flags/switches, and special leading characters. For PowerShell it also follows value-taking parameters (e.g. -Path) to grab their value.

list.title: pathParameterSchema
list.description: Resolve the override schema for a path parameter by name, with route-specific patterns for id/requestID on workspace, permission, and question routes.

list.title: payloads
list.description: Build Zod object schemas for every registered event, each annotated with an Event.<type> ref.

list.title: permissionHandlers
list.description: Registers the handlers for the "permission" HTTP API group on the instance API.

list.title: pint
list.description: Laravel Pint formatter for PHP files. enabled(context) requires laravel/pint listed in a composer.json up the tree.

list.title: plain
list.description: Convert a Sequelize model row to a plain object, or undefined when the row is null/undefined.

list.title: plain
list.description: Convert a Sequelize model instance to a plain object, or undefined when null.

list.title: planRequest
list.description: Compute the routing plan for a request, accounting for the env-pinned workspace, the selected workspace (session or query param), missing workspaces, and control-plane-only routes.

list.title: planWorkspaceRequest
list.description: Build a request plan for a resolved workspace: Remote when the target is remote (proxy), otherwise Local.

list.title: policy
list.description: Builds an Effect Schedule that retries while retryable returns a reason, waiting the computed delay between attempts and reporting progress.

list.title: powershellCommandSection
list.description: Build the PowerShell-specific "command section" of the tool description, covering notes, directory verification, quoting, output limits, and chaining.

list.title: powershellNotes
list.description: Return the PowerShell-specific usage notes block for the given PowerShell variant (pwsh 7+ or Windows PowerShell 5.1); empty for other shells.

list.title: prefix
list.description: Return the literal (non-glob) prefix of a token up to the first glob metacharacter (?, *, [); returns undefined if the token begins with a glob character.

list.title: preserveRecentBudget
list.description: Compute the token budget reserved for preserving recent turns: the configured value if set, otherwise 25% of usable context clamped to the min/max bounds.

list.title: prettier
list.description: Prettier formatter for web/JS/TS and many config/markup files. enabled(context) walks up from context.directory to context.worktree looking for a package.json that lists prettier as a (dev)dependency, then resolves the binary via npm.

list.title: preview
list.description: Produce a metadata-sized preview of output, keeping the tail and prefixing "..." when the text exceeds MAX_METADATA_LENGTH.

list.title: profile
list.description: Assemble the full set of prompt fragments (intro, workdir section, command section, git/PR guidance, parameter description) for the given shell and platform.

list.title: projectHandlers
list.description: Registers the handlers for the "project" HTTP API group on the instance API.

list.title: provideInstanceContext
list.description: Provide the instance context for effect based on the current workspace route. Reads the route's directory (URI-decoded) and workspace ID, then runs effect inside the corresponding instance from the store with the workspace ref provided.

list.title: provideLocal
list.description: Run downstream handlers inside WorkspaceContext.provide -> WithInstance.provide so that Effect services resolve against the given workspace and directory. The wrapped promise settles when the response finishes or errors.

list.title: provider
list.description: Selects the base system prompt(s) appropriate for the given model, choosing a Trinity- or Kimi-specific prompt by model id and otherwise the default.

list.title: provider
list.description: Strip a PowerShell provider qualifier from a path, returning the bare path only for the filesystem provider. Returns undefined for non-filesystem providers (e.g. Env:) and leaves drive-letter paths (C:) untouched.

list.title: providerHandlers
list.description: Registers the handlers for the "provider" HTTP API group on the instance API.

list.title: providerMeta
list.description: Strip the internal providerExecuted flag from part metadata, returning the remaining provider metadata or undefined when nothing is left.

list.title: proxyRemote
list.description: Proxy a request to a remote workspace's upstream target. Verifies the workspace is syncing (else 503), forwards method/headers/body faithfully, strips hop-by-hop headers, honors the sync fence (waiting for local catch-up), and streams the upstream body back to the client.

list.title: proxyRemote
list.description: Proxy a request to a remote workspace target, returning a 503 when the workspace's sync connection is broken. Routes WebSocket upgrades through the WebSocket proxy; otherwise proxies over HTTP, then honors any sync fence header in the response by waiting for the workspace to catch up (returning 503 on sync failure).

list.title: prune
list.description: Promise wrapper around the compaction service's prune operation.

list.title: ptyConnectRoute
list.description: Registers the GET WebSocket route that connects a client to a PTY: it upgrades the request to a socket, adapts the socket into a minimal WebSocket-like object so the Pty service can stream output, replays from an optional cursor, and forwards incoming socket messages as PTY input until the socket closes.

list.title: ptyHandlers
list.description: Builds the "pty" HTTP API handler group: shells/list/create/get/update/remove endpoints backed by the Pty service.

list.title: publish
list.description: Publish an mDNS/Bonjour "http" service advertising the server on the local network. No-op if the same port is already published; tears down any prior advertisement first.

list.title: queryBoolean
list.description: Coerce a query-string boolean ("true"/"false" or an actual boolean) into a boolean.

list.title: queryBoolean
list.description: Coerces a query-string boolean into a real boolean.

list.title: queryKeyJsonReplacer
list.description: Replacer that converts non-JSON values (bigint, Date, etc.) to safe substitutes.

list.title: questionHandlers
list.description: Builds the "question" HTTP API handler group, wiring list/reply/reject endpoints to the Question service.

list.title: raceAbort
list.description: Race an effect against an abort signal, interrupting the effect when the signal fires.

list.title: rawInstanceRoutes
list.description: Layer for raw (non-HttpApi) instance routes such as the PTY connect upgrade, behind the instance router middleware.

list.title: readManagedPreferences
list.description: On macOS, read MDM-deployed managed preferences for the closedcode domain by locating the managed .plist, converting it to JSON via plutil, and stripping payload metadata. No-op on non-macOS platforms.

list.title: registerOperation
list.description: Register a single OpenAPI operation for method path in the registry, resolving any Zod schemas in responses/requestBody to JSON Schema.

list.title: render
list.description: Render the complete shell tool definition for a given shell and platform: substitutes the profile fragments into the description template and builds the matching parameter schema.

list.title: renderPrompt
list.description: Substitute ${key} placeholders in a template with the matching value, throwing if any referenced key is missing.

list.title: replace
list.description: Replaces an occurrence of oldString with newString in content, trying each replacer strategy in order until one finds a match. Throws if oldString equals newString, if no replacer locates the text, or (when not replacing all) if the located text is ambiguous (multiple occurrences).

list.title: reportValidation
list.description: Middleware that reports express-validator field-chain failures using the same 400 envelope as the Zod validator; passes through when there are no errors.

list.title: requestAttributes
list.description: Build OpenTelemetry span attributes from an Express request (HTTP method/path plus matched route params).

list.title: requestAttributes
list.description: Build OpenTelemetry span attributes from an Express request (HTTP method/path plus matched route params).

list.title: requestAttributes
list.description: Builds OTel span attributes from an Express request: HTTP method, path, and every matched route param.

list.title: requestAttributes
list.description: Builds OTel span attributes from an Express request: HTTP method, mounted path, and every matched route param.

list.title: requestAttributes
list.description: Builds OTel span attributes from an Express request: HTTP method, path, and every matched route param.

list.title: requestAttributes
list.description: Builds OTel span attributes from an Express request: HTTP method, path, and every matched route param.

list.title: requestAttributes
list.description: Builds OTel span attributes from an Express request: HTTP method, path, and every matched route param.

list.title: requestAttributes
list.description: Builds OTel span attributes from an Express request: HTTP method, mounted path, and every matched route param.

list.title: requestAttributes
list.description: Builds OTel span attributes from an Express request: HTTP method, path, and every matched route param.

list.title: requestAttributes
list.description: Builds OTel span attributes from an Express request: HTTP method, path, and every matched route param.

list.title: requestAttributes
list.description: Builds the OTel span attributes for an Express request (method, path, and route params).

list.title: requestAttributes
list.description: Build the base span attributes for an HTTP request: HTTP method, path, and every matched route param.

list.title: requestURL
list.description: Build an absolute URL for a request, used for workspace routing decisions.

list.title: requestURL
list.description: Parse a request URL into a URL, using a localhost base for relative request URLs.

list.title: requiresExtensionsForCustomServers
list.description: For custom (non-builtin) LSP server entries, extensions is required so the client knows which files the server should attach to. Builtin server IDs and explicitly disabled entries are exempt.

list.title: resolve
list.description: Convert a Zod schema to a JSON Schema fragment for OpenAPI. If the schema carries a ref via .meta({ref}), it is registered under components/schemas and a $ref is returned instead of the inline schema.

list.title: resolveRequestBody
list.description: Resolve every content schema inside an OpenAPI requestBody, converting Zod schemas to JSON Schema.

list.title: resolveTarget
list.description: Resolve the routing target for a workspace via its project/type adapter (e.g. local directory or remote URL).

list.title: resolveTools
list.description: Filter the available tools down to those allowed for this request: drops tools disabled by merged agent/request permissions and tools the user message explicitly turned off.

list.title: resolveWasm
list.description: Resolve a tree-sitter wasm asset reference to a plain filesystem path, accepting file:// URLs, absolute paths, and module-relative references.

list.title: resolveWorkspace
list.description: Look up a workspace by ID unless routing is pinned by an env workspace ID.

list.title: resolveWorkspaceRoute
list.description: Resolve the effective workspace + adapter target for an incoming request URL, mirroring InstanceMiddleware / planRequest. Pure resolution only — performs no proxying and no response writes. Used by both the HTTP middleware and the WebSocket upgrade handler so the selection logic lives in one place.

list.title: responseDescription
list.description: Build an OpenAPI annotation that overrides the description of an endpoint's 200 response.

list.title: retryable
list.description: Decides whether an error should be retried and, if so, the human-readable reason. Treats 5xx and rate-limit-style errors (via status code, message heuristics, or parsed JSON error codes) as retryable; context overflow is not.

list.title: rewriteRefs
list.description: Recursively rewrite every $ref pointing at the from component to point at the to component.

list.title: rlang
list.description: air formatter for R files. enabled() confirms the air binary is the R language server/formatter via its --help output.

list.title: root
list.description: Base URL path for the permission route group.

list.title: root
list.description: Base URL path for the project route group.

list.title: root
list.description: Base URL path for the provider route group.

list.title: root
list.description: Base URL path for the PTY route group.

list.title: root
list.description: Base URL path prefix for all question endpoints.

list.title: root
list.description: Base URL path prefix for all session endpoints.

list.title: root
list.description: Base URL path prefix for all sync endpoints.

list.title: root
list.description: Base URL path prefix for all TUI endpoints.

list.title: root
list.description: Base URL path prefix for all experimental workspace endpoints.

list.title: rootApiRoutes
list.description: Layer building the root (non-instance) API routes from control-plane and global handlers.

list.title: routeHttpApiWorkspace
list.description: Route an HttpApi request by resolving the session's workspace (when a session is referenced), planning the request, and executing the plan.

list.title: routeWorkspace
list.description: Execute a request plan: respond with a not-found error, proxy to the remote target, or run the local handler effect with the WorkspaceRouteContext provided.

list.title: routes
list.description: The default route layer built with no custom CORS options.

list.title: rubocop
list.description: rubocop formatter for Ruby files. Enabled when rubocop is on PATH.

list.title: ruff
list.description: ruff formatter for Python files. enabled(context) requires ruff on PATH plus either a ruff config (with [tool.ruff] when in pyproject.toml) or a dependency manifest that mentions ruff.

list.title: runEffect
list.description: Run an Effect inside a named tracing span carrying the request's attributes.

list.title: runRequest
list.description: Wraps an Effect in a span carrying the request attributes and runs it through AppRuntime.

list.title: runRequest
list.description: Runs an Effect inside a named span carrying the request attributes.

list.title: runRequest
list.description: Runs an Effect inside a named span carrying the request attributes.

list.title: runRequest
list.description: Runs an Effect inside an OTel span built from the request.

list.title: runRequest
list.description: Runs an Effect inside a named OTel span carrying the request attributes.

list.title: runRequest
list.description: Run an effect as a traced HTTP request, wrapping it in a span named name with the request's attributes.

list.title: runRequestExpress
list.description: Run an Effect inside a named tracing span carrying the request's attributes.

list.title: runtime
list.description: Router middleware that selects the server backend and annotates the current span with backend attributes before running each handler.

list.title: rustfmt
list.description: rustfmt formatter for Rust files. Enabled when rustfmt is on PATH.

list.title: sanitize
list.description: Remove hop-by-hop and internal routing headers from a Headers object in place. Strips the hop-by-hop set plus accept-encoding and the x-closedcode/x-opencode directory/workspace headers.

list.title: select
list.description: Select the server backend to use.

list.title: selectedWorkspaceID
list.description: Determine the workspace ID selected for a request, preferring the session's workspace over the workspace query param.

list.title: serializeQueryKeyValue
list.description: Normalizes any accepted value into a JSON-friendly shape for query keys.

list.title: serializeSearchParams
list.description: Turns URLSearchParams into a sorted JSON object for deterministic keys.

list.title: serveDocs
list.description: Mount the swagger-ui docs UI on the Express app at the given path, serving the built spec.

list.title: serveUI
list.description: Serves the embedded web UI asset matching the request path (falling back to index.html). Sets the content type and, for HTML, the default CSP header.

list.title: serveUIEffect
list.description: Serve a static file from the embedded web UI bundle for the given request. Resolves the request path against the embedded manifest (falling back to index.html), reads the matched file, and returns it with the appropriate content-type and CSP headers. Returns 404 when the asset is missing and 503 when the build has no embedded web UI.

list.title: sessionCursor
list.description: Codec for the session pagination cursor: a base64url-encoded JSON blob carrying a session id, its creation time, the sort order, the paging direction, and the active list filters (so subsequent pages keep them).

list.title: sessionCursor.decode
list.description: Decodes a base64url cursor string back into its validated cursor object.

list.title: sessionCursor.encode
list.description: Encodes a session, paging metadata, and the active filters into an opaque base64url cursor string.

list.title: sessionHandlers
list.description: Builds the "session" HTTP API handler group: the full set of session lifecycle, message, prompt, share, revert, permission, and part endpoints backed by the session-related services.

list.title: sessionHandlers
list.description: Builds the "v2.session" HTTP API handler group: sessions (paginated list), prompt, compact, wait, and context endpoints.

list.title: shellDisplayName
list.description: Map an internal shell name to a human-readable display name.

list.title: shfmt
list.description: shfmt formatter for shell scripts. Enabled when shfmt is on PATH.

list.title: shouldStayOnControlPlane
list.description: Decide whether a request must be handled on the control plane regardless of workspace selection (local workspace routes and /console paths).

list.title: skills
list.description: Builds the available-skills section of the system prompt for the given agent, or returns nothing when the skill permission is disabled.

list.title: sliceAfterMatch
list.description: Return the portion of a (forward-slash-normalized) path that follows the first matching search root, or undefined when no root matches.

list.title: source
list.description: Return the trimmed source text of a command node, preferring the enclosing redirected statement so redirections are included in the captured command.

list.title: source
list.description: Resolve the source identifier (used for error reporting) of a substitution input.

list.title: splitTurn
list.description: Find the earliest split point inside a single turn whose suffix [start, turn.end) fits within the remaining token budget, so part of an oversized turn can still be preserved in the tail.

list.title: sqlite
list.description: Builds a synchronous SessionMessageUpdater adapter backed by sequelize. It pre-loads the assistant/compaction/shell messages for the session, exposes getters/setters the updater can call synchronously, and queues all writes so they can be flushed (in order) after the updater finishes.

list.title: stableSchema
list.description: Produce a stable, comparable string for a schema by canonicalizing it (sorted keys, descriptions dropped, refs canonicalized).

list.title: standardrb
list.description: standardrb formatter for Ruby files. Enabled when standardrb is on PATH.

list.title: stop
list.description: Stop the server, resolving once it has fully closed. Idempotent.

list.title: stream
list.description: Async-iterate all messages of a session in chronological (oldest-first) order, paging through the database in fixed-size batches.

list.title: streamEvents
list.description: Streams server-sent events to the client: sets SSE headers, sends an initial connected event plus periodic heartbeats, pumps an AsyncQueue to the response, and tears everything down when the request closes.

list.title: stringifyToJsonValue
list.description: Safely stringifies a value and parses it back into a JsonValue.

list.title: stripOptionalNull
list.description: Strip {type:"null"} arms that Effect's Schema.optional adds to OpenAPI unions, recursing into allOf/anyOf/oneOf, items, properties, and additionalProperties, and collapsing single-arm unions.

list.title: submitTuiRequest
list.description: Enqueues a TUI request for a connected TUI to pull.

list.title: submitTuiResponse
list.description: Enqueues a TUI response for the originating caller waiting in callTui.

list.title: substitute
list.description: Apply {env:VAR} and {file:path} substitutions to config text. {env:VAR} is replaced by the environment variable's value (empty string if unset). {file:path} is replaced by the JSON-escaped trimmed contents of the referenced file; relative paths resolve against the config directory, ~/ expands to the home directory, and tokens on lines starting with // are left untouched. Missing files either throw an InvalidError or expand to empty depending on input.missing.

list.title: summaryText
list.description: Concatenate the trimmed text parts of a message into a single summary string.

list.title: syncHandlers
list.description: Builds the "sync" HTTP API handler group: start/replay/history endpoints for cross-instance event sync.

list.title: systemManagedConfigDir
list.description: Resolve the OS-specific system directory for managed (MDM-deployed) closedcode config.

list.title: tail
list.description: Keep only the tail of the output within the given line and byte budgets, truncating a final overlong line mid-character on a UTF-8 boundary.

list.title: terraform
list.description: terraform formatter for Terraform files. Enabled when terraform is on PATH.

list.title: toKind
list.description: Normalize an arbitrary shell name to a known kind, falling back to "bash".

list.title: toModelMessages
list.description: Promise wrapper around toModelMessagesEffect that runs it with the logger layer.

list.title: toModelMessagesEffect
list.description: Convert stored session messages into provider model messages. Maps each part type to its model representation: user text/file/compaction/subtask parts, assistant text/reasoning/step-start parts, and tool parts (completed, error, and interrupted pending/running calls, which are forced to errors so every tool_use has a matching tool_result as Anthropic/Claude APIs require). Media from tool results is split into a synthetic follow-up user message when the provider can't carry media in tool results. Optionally strips media and truncates tool output for compaction.

list.title: toPartialRow
list.description: Maps a partial session-update info object to a partial sqlite row, flattening nested share/summary/time fields to their column names and dropping any keys whose value resolved to undefined (so absent fields aren't overwritten).

list.title: traceContext
list.description: Adapts an Express request into the minimal context shape trace.js expects.

list.title: trimDiff
list.description: Removes the common leading indentation shared by all content lines (+/-/space) of a unified diff, leaving the +/-/space prefixes and the ---/+++ header lines untouched, so the diff renders without a large uniform indent.

list.title: truncateToolOutput
list.description: Truncate tool output to a maximum character count for compaction, appending a marker noting how many characters were omitted. Returns the text unchanged when maxChars is falsy or the text already fits.

list.title: tuiHandlers
list.description: Builds the "tui" HTTP API handler group: endpoints that publish TUI control events onto the bus.

list.title: turns
list.description: Split a message list into conversational turns, where each turn starts at a non-compaction user message and ends at the next turn's start (or list end).

list.title: uiRoute
list.description: Catch-all UI route layer that serves the web UI (and proxies dev assets) behind authorization middleware.

list.title: unpublish
list.description: Tear down the active mDNS/Bonjour advertisement (if any) and reset internal state.

list.title: unquote
list.description: Strip a single matching pair of surrounding single or double quotes.

list.title: unquoteGitPath
list.description: Decodes a git "C-quoted" path (a path git wraps in double quotes with backslash/octal escapes) back into its real UTF-8 form. Returns the input unchanged when it is not a quoted path.

list.title: update
list.description: Applies a single session-message event to the store: builds the adapter, runs the synchronous updater against it, then flushes the queued writes.

list.title: usable
list.description: Compute the usable input-token budget for a model, reserving headroom for the response. Uses the model's explicit input limit minus a reserved buffer when available, otherwise the context window minus max output tokens.

list.title: uvformat
list.description: uv formatter for Python files, used only when ruff is not already enabled. enabled(context) checks uv is on PATH and supports format via its --help output.

list.title: v2Handlers
list.description: Combined v2 handlers layer: merges the v2 session and message handler groups and provides the SessionV2 default layer.

list.title: validateCredential
list.description: Run effect only if auth is not required or the credential is authorized; otherwise fail with an Unauthorized HttpApi error.

list.title: validateRawCredential
list.description: Router-level credential check: pass through effect when auth is not required or the credential is authorized, otherwise short-circuit with an empty 401 response.

list.title: validator
list.description: Build middleware that validates the chosen request segment against a Zod schema. On success the parsed value is stored on req.valid[target]; on failure it responds 400 with { data, errors, success: false }.

list.title: wait
list.description: Run waitEffect on the app runtime, blocking until the workspace reaches the target state.

list.title: waitEffect
list.description: Build an Effect that blocks until the given workspace has synced to the target state.

list.title: waitForAbort
list.description: Build an effect that interrupts when the given abort signal fires.

list.title: webHandler
list.description: Get a web (Fetch-style) request handler for the server. Returns the memoized default handler when no CORS options are supplied; otherwise builds a fresh handler with the given CORS options (using a non-shared memo map so the default route memoization is not reused).

list.title: webSource
list.description: Extract the underlying web Request from a server request, if present.

list.title: websocket
list.description: Proxy a WebSocket upgrade request to a target URL, bidirectionally piping messages between client and target. Forwards close events in both directions and translates socket errors into close frames.

list.title: websocketProtocols
list.description: Parse the requested WebSocket subprotocols from the sec-websocket-protocol header.

list.title: websocketTargetURL
list.description: Convert an http(s) URL into its WebSocket (ws/wss) equivalent for proxying.

list.title: workspaceHandlers
list.description: Builds the "workspace" HTTP API handler group: adapters/list/create/status/remove/sessionRestore endpoints.

list.title: workspaceProxyURL
list.description: Build the upstream URL used to proxy a request to a remote workspace target. Joins the target's base path with the request pathname, carries over the request search/hash, and strips the internal workspace query parameter.

list.title: workspaceRouterMiddleware
list.description: Router-level workspace routing middleware for raw (non-HttpApi) routes; plans and routes each request without session lookup.

list.title: workspaceRoutingLayer
list.description: Layer implementing the WorkspaceRoutingMiddleware HttpApi service, wiring the WebSocket constructor, Workspace service, and HTTP client into routing.

list.title: wrap
list.description: Wraps a tool's resolved info so its execute() decodes/validates arguments against the tool's parameter schema, applies output truncation (unless the result already reports a truncation state), and records a tracing span.

list.title: zig
list.description: zig formatter for Zig files. Enabled when zig is on PATH.
